- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-19b found that the four converging lines in §"The Neuroscience of Deliberate Practice" cite Petersen 1998, Poldrack 2001, Wolpert & Flanagan 2001, Zatorre 2007, Walker 2002, Fischer 2002, Robertson 2004, Bengtsson 2005, and Ericsson 1993 — all 19–33 years old in a domain where the 2014–2024 critical literature has reshaped the consensus. Specifically: (a) the Ericsson deliberate-practice tradition has been contested (Hambrick et al. 2014, 2016, 2020; Ericsson 2019 response); (b) sleep-consolidation has a mixed replication record (Pan & Rickard 2015 meta-analysis is the canonical cautionary note); (c) the Poldrack 2001 prefrontal-to-striatal handoff has been re-described in some 2020s work as parallel-stream activation rather than sequential transfer. The article's last_deep_review is 2026-04-28; the literature-drift cadence is weekly Tuesday 05:00 UTC and this article is overdue for a targeted pass. Fix: queue a `literature-drift-review` targeting this article specifically (in addition to its standard weekly rotation). The drift review should retrieve 2014–2024 work on the three named threads and recommend specific citation refreshes. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19b.md` Issue 5.

- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-02 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro), methodology improvement items 11, 12, 14, 15. Recommendations to capture as a named discipline: (a) DOI-first citation verification — for every cited source, verify author names / year / title / venue / DOI / correction & retraction notices against the LIVE external record, not the intra-corpus copy; (b) run a distinct external-source audit pass after every deep-review on articles carrying scientific citations, corrected papers, or active theoretical debates (the literature-drift-review cadence is the natural home — assess whether to fold a DOI-verify step into it); (c) a quotation-discipline rule (any quoted sentence must be exact + page/section-located; paraphrases must not sit in quote marks); (d) a "tenet-use guardrail" — when an article's "Relation to Site Perspective" section invokes dualism/irreducibility/bidirectional interaction, require one sentence stating what a non-dualist rival could also explain, preventing convenient bracketing while preserving the declared stance. Assess-first: some of this may already be covered by the calibration-audit-triple / literature-drift discipline — extend the existing doc rather than duplicating (see [[stale-expand-tasks-may-be-redundant]]); declining redundant parts is valid. Target the calibration-audit-triple project doc or a sibling methodology doc; this is project-doc work, not site content.

### ✓ 2026-06-02: Develop the multidimensionality of "minimal" across concepts/minimal-consciousness.md and voids/minimal-consciousness-void.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-02 (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro), findings §1/§3 and improvement items 9, 10. The "lower bound" / "basement level" metaphor implies a single one-dimensional scale, but contemporary work (Bayne/Hohwy/Owen on multidimensional global states; Birch/Schnell/Clayton on animal-consciousness dimensions) separates wakefulness, content, selfhood, integration, valence, access, and reportability — there may be many *minimalities*, not one minimum. This does not dissolve the void; it changes its shape, and arguably STRENGTHENS the Map's case (more axes that each resist a clean threshold). Add a short passage treating "minimal" as potentially plural in BOTH the void article and [[concepts/minimal-consciousness]] (multi-file task — do not drop the concept page; see [[multifile-task-dropped-file]]). Also tighten the cross-link to [[voids/edge-states-and-void-probes]], importing its transcendence-vs-edge-mapping tension so cessation reads as an *ambiguous probe* rather than near-confirmation of an inaccessible lower edge. Web-verify the Bayne/Hohwy/Owen and Birch et al. attributions before citing ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]); these are NEW citations the reviewer suggested, not existing ones, so do not import them unverified. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] — present multidimensionality as a live framing that reshapes the void, not as established fact. Confirm cap headroom on concepts/ before any growth.

(2) ADD MPE-92M STUDY LIMITATIONS to the paragraph around line 55 so the "search keeps finding more than nothing" point doesn't over-read a contested instrument: the 12-factor solution explained ~44% of total variance, item-specific variance remained large, reproducibility was not guaranteed, model fit had weaknesses, and the sample was not representative (per the Gamma & Metzinger paper's own limitations). (3) SCOPE THE IIT EXAMPLE (line 61-63): the "photodiode with Φ > 0 qualifies" framing is loose early-IIT; note that IIT 4.0 uses a more developed substrate/maximal-integrated-information account, so either historically scope the photodiode example to early IIT or update it, and attribute the unfalsifiability charge to specific critics rather than "some scholars." ### ✓ 2026-06-02: Fix MPE-92M citation misattribution in voids/minimal-consciousness-void.md (Winter → Gamma & Metzinger) — VERIFIED
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-02, 2/3 reviewers independently verified): [chatgpt, claude]. Already P1 (no upgrade beyond P1). Both ChatGPT and Claude live-fetched the PLOS ONE article and independently confirmed the misattribution: Claude — "the MPE-92M questionnaire is Gamma & Metzinger (2021), not 'Winter et al. (2021)'—a fabricated first author"; ChatGPT — "the PLOS ONE article is by Alex Gamma and Thomas Metzinger... there is no author named Winter." (Gemini reproduced "Winter et al." in its own report and did NOT catch the error — correlated with the article's defect, not an independent confirmation.) This is the strongest convergent finding of the cycle: two reviewers verified the same concrete factual defect against the live source. The one CONFIRMED factual defect. The article attributes the MPE-92M questionnaire to "Winter, U., et al. (2021)" at body line 55 ("...(Winter et al. 2021)") and in reference 6. This is WRONG. Live-verified against the PLOS ONE article during outer-review processing (WebFetch of id 10.1371/journal.pone.0253694): the authors are **Alex Gamma and Thomas Metzinger**, "The Minimal Phenomenal Experience questionnaire (MPE-92M): Towards a phenomenological profile of 'pure awareness' experiences in meditators," *PLOS ONE* 16(7): e0253694 (July 14 2021). There is NO author named Winter on the paper. PLOS also issued a 2024 correction: Gamma & Metzinger (2024), *PLOS ONE* 19(11): e0314290 (data-availability correction) — add a brief note of the correction. This is exactly the propagated-AI-citation-metadata failure class (see [[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]): the Map's own 2026-05-26 deep-review explicitly asserted this citation was accurate, so internal cross-checking missed it; live external verification caught it. FIX: replace both the inline "(Winter et al. 2021)" and reference 6 with the correct Gamma & Metzinger attribution + DOI + 2024-correction note. While in the file, grep the corpus (incl. research/) for any other "Winter" MPE-92M propagation and repoint. Do NOT change the surrounding argument. Tenet alignment unaffected.

### ✓ 2026-06-02: Add overlooked probing strategies + downgrade "unexplorable" to "partially probe-able / contested" in voids/minimal-consciousness-void.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-02, 2/3 reviewers): [claude, chatgpt]. CONVERGENT — Claude (Dimension 3) argues "unexplorable + occluded" is overstated and should be downgraded to "partially probe-able / contested"; ChatGPT (§3 + improvement items 7, 8) independently flags the same gap: "the article should distinguish introspective impossibility from scientific impossibility... no-report paradigms, anaesthesia research, sleep studies, animal consciousness studies, and neural complexity measures all attempt to investigate consciousness without relying solely on reflective verbal report... these methods may underdetermine the answer, but they are not nothing," recommending a "Direct vs Indirect Access" subsection. Coordinate with the ChatGPT modal-overreach task (which adds that same Direct-vs-Indirect distinction) so the no-report passage is written once. The reviewer argues "unexplorable + occluded" is overstated: the void survives *concurrent first-person introspection* but does not engage non-concurrent and third-person probes that target exactly the declared gap. Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit: the right move is to honestly mark the framework-boundary — concede direct concurrent introspection is genuinely (perhaps structurally) blocked, but acknowledge the minimal state is approachable by routes that *constrain* rather than *establish* its character. Concrete edits: (a) add a passage engaging no-report paradigms (Tsuchiya, Wilke, Frässle & Lamme 2015, *Trends in Cognitive Sciences* 19(12):757-770 — bypasses the contaminating report step), retrospective/non-concurrent Descriptive Experience Sampling (Hurlburt — minimises rather than eliminates concurrent load), dreamless-sleep and edge-state probes (Windt, Nielsen & Thompson 2016, *TiCS* 20(12):871-882), and third-person cessation-EEG; (b) reclassify the void from "unexplorable" to "partially probe-able / contested" and mark "occluded" as the least-earned label (it rests on the conditional "if consciousness is fundamental," which is a tenet, not a finding). Web-verify every NEW citation's authors/year/venue/stance before insertion ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]) — do NOT import unverified. This is a substantive editorial disagreement with the classification, not an automatic correction; preserve the genuine concurrent-introspection limit. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]. Coordinate with the sibling same-file refine tasks to avoid same-session churn ([[refine-then-condense-same-session-churn]]). Likely converges with a Gemini same-date finding — expect /combine-outer-reviews to upgrade.

### ✓ 2026-06-02: Engage the named deflationary competitors (HOT, Global Workspace, Illusionism) in voids/minimal-consciousness-void.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-02, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. CONVERGENT — all three independently flagged that the article leaves its central premise unprotected against the dominant in-framework explanations of the "introspective void." Gemini (weakness 5): the text "completely ignores Illusionism... the void is merely the functional shadow of the brain's data-compression architecture" and HOT, under which "the 'added content' [of introspection] is the very thing that turns a biologically unconscious physiological state into a conscious experience." Claude (Dimension 1/4): "a physicalist—indeed an illusionist like Dennett or a higher-order theorist—can run the identical observer-effect argument," so the article "does not engage the strongest opposing position." ChatGPT (§3): "higher-order and global-workspace-style theories deserve explicit engagement... reportability, availability, access, or metacognitive monitoring are not merely contaminating additions; they are part of what makes a state conscious." A boundary-substitution finding NOT subsumed by the ChatGPT generic "What Would Challenge This View?" section — this task names the specific competitors. Verified by reading the article: it engages **none** of the three dominant deflationary frameworks that explain the "introspective void" without an irreducible lower bound — Higher-Order Thought (HOT) theory (the void = absence of a higher-order representation; introspective "enrichment" is the very thing that makes a state conscious, so demanding a pre-enrichment conscious state is asking to see a square circle), Global Workspace Theory (the lower bound = the minimum threshold of global broadcast; failure to introspect below it = failure to broadcast weak signals), and Illusionism (the void = the resolution limit of the brain's self-monitoring; a computational blind spot reified into metaphysics). Apply the [[direct-refutation-discipline]] remit. This is the classic boundary-substitution failure mode three independent outer reviewers have converged on: a competing in-framework explanation is available and goes unengaged. The right move is a mix of (a) in-framework engagement where the deflation can be met on its own terms — e.g. HOT and the void's thesis are partly *complementary* (both agree introspection adds structure; they disagree on whether anything conscious exists below it), so state precisely where the disagreement bites rather than ignoring HOT; and (b) honest framework-boundary-marking where it cannot — illusionism denies the explanandum (phenomenal "what-it's-like") the Map takes as given under [[tenets#^dualism|dualism]], so name that as a bedrock disagreement, NOT a refutation. Do NOT let the article's existing dualist commitment silently *close* the question against these competitors; present them as live alternatives the void is underdetermined between, then state the Map's reasons for its reading. Add a compact "Deflationary Alternatives" passage (or fold into the planned "What Would Challenge This View?" section from the ChatGPT task — coordinate to avoid duplication). Apply reply modes in natural journal-quality prose ([[writing-style|writing-style guide]]); do NOT expose mode labels in the body — classification goes in the changelog. Web-verify any NEW citation (Frankish on illusionism; Rosenthal/Lau on HOT; Dehaene/Mashour on GWT; Sandved-Smith et al. 2025 "deep computational neurophenomenology" if used) for authors/year/venue/stance before insertion ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]) — do NOT import unverified. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]. Coordinate with the sibling same-file 2026-06-02 refine cluster (esp. the ChatGPT "What Would Challenge This View?" task) — one editor pass strongly preferred to avoid same-session churn ([[refine-then-condense-same-session-churn]]). Strong convergence candidate — expect /combine-outer-reviews to upgrade.

(2) DOCTRINAL-CONSISTENCY: line 137 imports Hasker's EMERGENT (from-below, brain-generates-mind) dualism approvingly into the unity argument without marking that it differs from the Map's bi-aspectual CO-FUNDAMENTAL view on the dependence direction — add a half-sentence marking the divergence ([[the-strong-emergence-of-consciousness]] is careful about this; this article is not). ### ✓ 2026-06-02: Repair partial boundary-substitution in the Anscombe reply — topics/argument-from-reason.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-02 ([[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-02-argument-from-reason]]) found a P1 partial boundary-substitution at lines 129–131. The reply to Anscombe collapses her compatibilism toward epiphenomenalism via the causal-closure tenet ("Anscombe's reconciliation works only if we abandon causal closure—which is to abandon physicalism"), but this presupposes the causal-EXCLUSION reading that the live in-framework opponent — non-reductive physicalism — explicitly denies (reasons-explanation and neural-explanation true of the SAME event under different descriptions; no second cause competing, so closure is untouched). The in-framework refutation (Kim's exclusion argument vs. Davidsonian/Yablo non-reductive replies) IS available and is the actual 2020s debate; the article substitutes the tenet boundary for it and never names non-reductive physicalism. Per [[direct-refutation-discipline]]: UPGRADE to the in-framework exclusion-argument engagement — show why the "same event under two descriptions" answer fails to give reasons genuine causal purchase (the supervenient description inherits its causal completeness from the physical base, so reasons do no ADDITIONAL causal work — the epiphenomenalist worry now EARNED rather than assumed). That converts the boundary appeal into a genuine in-framework dilemma. STRICT: no editor-mode labels in article prose (no "boundary-substitution", "in-framework", "direct-refutation-feasible", etc.). Preserve the philosophical substance; remove the tenet-appeal dressing.

P., et al." but the real authors are **Rouleau, N. & Cimino, N.** (web-verified: MDPI mdpi.com/2673-4087/3/3/32, PMID 39483436, PMC11523760, DOI 10.3390/neurosci3030032); (2) WRONG YEAR — cited as 2024 but published **2022** (NeuroSci vol 3(3) = 2022; the scorer-independent volume/year check flags it); (3) SOURCE-CONCLUSION MISMATCH (the load-bearing one) — article line ~98 body prose says "…what **Bhatt et al. (2024) call the 'core elements' re-engaging one by one**" as a second source corroborating Mashour (2024) on anaesthesia EMERGENCE — but the Rouleau & Cimino paper is about the brain as an EM-field RECEIVER (transmissive vs productive model), it says NOTHING about emergence "core elements re-engaging." That claim is mis-attributed; either reattribute the "core elements re-engaging" claim to its ACTUAL source (likely Mashour 2024 itself, who is already cited in the same sentence, OR a genuine anaesthesia-emergence paper — web-verify) or remove the unsupported "—what Bhatt et al. call…" clause. FIX (a) the reference entry (line ~185) author+year → Rouleau, N. & Cimino, N. (2022); (b) the body line ~98 attribution. PROPAGATION ROOT: the wrong "Bhatt et al. (2024)" attribution originated in **obsidian/research/consciousness-anesthesia-filter-theory-2026-03-20.md** (lines 57, 191, 221 — has the correct PMC11523760 but wrong author/year) — FIX THE NOTE BODY THERE TOO (not just References) or it re-propagates ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]] point 12/20). ### ✓ 2026-06-01: Fix evidential-status overreach and MWI boundary-substitution in biological-computationalisms-inadvertent-case-for-dualism
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-01 found the body is well-converged (the "structural rather than evidential" framing in the lede is exemplary and must be preserved verbatim) but the "Relation to Site Perspective" tenet section drops that discipline. THREE findings, all in that section. (1) HIGH — EVIDENTIAL-STATUS OVERREACH, Minimal Quantum Interaction subsection (§92): cites avian magnetoreception and photosynthetic energy transfer as if they corroborate "a quantum-level interface for consciousness." These are femtosecond-to-microsecond effects in specifically-evolved molecular machinery with no demonstrated bearing on the dendritic/ion-flow/field dynamics named elsewhere, let alone on experience. A possibility ("could be") dressed as demonstrated-phenomena support; physicalism-compatible facts presented as dualism-supporting. FIX: either cut the magnetoreception/photosynthesis parenthetical and reframe as where the Map *locates* (not evidences) its proposed interface, OR explicitly mark the quantum-bio examples as defeater-removal ("quantum effects aren't impossible in warm wet biology") NOT positive evidence. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] epistemic-vs-metaphysical split. (2) HIGH (critical per [[direct-refutation-discipline]]) — BOUNDARY-SUBSTITUTION plus a factual defect, No Many Worlds subsection (§96): claims MWI "cannot honour the very substrate dependence" — staged as in-framework defeat of the Many-Worlds defender — but the premise "MWI multiplies substrates without constraint" MISDESCRIBES Everettian QM (each branch IS a fully substrate-specific trajectory; branching duplicates substrate with full specificity, it does not abstract it away). The reasoning actually rests on the Map's indexical-identity commitment, so it asserts the Map's perimeter while dressed as in-framework refutation. FIX: downgrade to honest framework-boundary marking — substrate specificity makes the indexical question vivid, and the Map's rejection of Many-Worlds rests on its foundational commitment that indexical identity is not duplicable, honestly noted as running counter to the Everettian's commitment, NOT refuted inside the Everettian's framework. Remove the false premise. DO NOT name editor modes in prose. (3) MEDIUM — UNSUPPORTED FOUNDATIONAL MOVE, Bidirectional Interaction subsection (§94): "any non-physical influence... would be computationally relevant rather than epiphenomenal" treats the non-epiphenomenality result as established on a borrowed-and-unearned conditional (the antecedent "the algorithm is the substrate" is Milinkovic & Aru's contested claim). FIX: reframe as doubly-conditional and tenet-flagged (IF the interactionist tenet holds AND IF biological computation tightly couples algorithm to substrate, THEN the influence would be computationally relevant) — an observation about where the mechanism would act, not a defeat of epiphenomenalism. ALSO: the frontmatter `description` ("raising questions that parallel central dualist arguments") slightly over-promises relative to the body's downgrade; align it. Citation web-verify (reports-only flags carried forward): Thagard (2022) *Philosophy of Science* 89(1):70-88 appears nowhere else in the corpus (corpus only cites Thagard 1992 elsewhere) — verify title/volume/pagination; Milinkovic & Aru (2026) *Neuroscience & Biobehavioral Reviews* 181, 106524 carries a specific epub/print split — confirm it exists and says "the algorithm is the substrate" since the whole article hangs on it. PRESERVE: the lede's structural-not-evidential framing, the Duch substrate-independent counter-example (§48/§74, a genuine virtue), the honest §84 admission that the regress argument "presupposes what it seeks to establish." See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-06-01-biological-computationalism]]. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism), Tenet 2 (Minimal Quantum Interaction — the §92 fix), Tenet 4 (No Many Worlds — the §96 fix).

### ✓ 2026-06-01: Reframe "the Map's emergentist interactionism" doctrinal slippage in parsimony-case-for-interactionist-dualism.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Surfaced by the 2026-06-01 deep-review of [[the-strong-emergence-of-consciousness]], which fixed the same slippage (the Map's view presented AS strong emergence / "emergentist interactionism", contradicting the canonical **bi-aspectual CO-FUNDAMENTAL dualism** — consciousness and structure are co-fundamental, NOT consciousness emerging "from below" out of physical complexity). DOCTRINAL/PROSE reframing, not a metadata swap. The slippage is at **line ~133**: "**The Map's emergentist interactionism** couples genuine ontological novelty to a specific physical interface" and "This is the strong emergence move articulated in detail elsewhere on the Map: consciousness possesses properties not deducible even in principle from physical facts...". FIX: (a) "the Map's emergentist interactionism" → "the Map's interactionist dualism" (drop the misleading "emergentist", which implies from-below emergence the Map rejects); (b) reframe "This is the strong emergence move... on the Map" so the Map is shown to SHARE strong emergence's irreducibility claim while grounding it in CO-FUNDAMENTAL rather than from-below ontology (mirror the "decisive amendment" paragraph just added to [[the-strong-emergence-of-consciousness]] and the [[concepts/emergence]] realignment). **Line ~74 is CORRECT and must NOT be touched** — it attributes "strongly emerges from physical processes" to TYPE-B PHYSICALISTS as a foil (correct), not to the Map. Preserve the parsimony argument; length-neutral. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]; the co-fundamental/from-below distinction is the load-bearing fix. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — the Map's view IS co-fundamental dualism, not emergentism).

- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From [[reviews/deep-review-2026-06-01-eliminative-materialism]]. Web-verified (Frontiers DOI 10.3389/fpsyg.2024.1449314): **Loginov, "Illusionism, Moore, and Chalmers" (Frontiers in Psychology 15, 2024) argues the Moorean argument against illusionism does NOT succeed** (the Qualitative Thesis lacks common-sense status). Multiple corpus files describe Loginov as "rehabilitating" / "attempting to rehabilitate" the Moorean argument or as arguing "the pre-theoretical certainty of pain survives" — this is BACKWARDS. Grep `obsidian/` for `Loginov` and `rehabilitate` (research/ illusionism note + concepts) and reconcile against the ONE correct location ("Loginov (2024) argued this Moorean argument doesn't succeed"). The "defends the Moorean argument" role almost certainly belongs to **Morozov (2025), "In Defense of Moorean Argument Against Illusionism," in Kasavin (ed.), *Revolution and Evolution*, RSHPS Moscow, pp. 397-402** — likely absent from the notes and conflated into Loginov. Also check the same "In Defence of the Moorean Argument... Frontiers 2024" title/venue FUSION did not propagate elsewhere (it was fixed in topics/eliminative-materialism.md this pass). Web-verify, do not corpus-vote.

### ✓ 2026-06-01: Address argumentative gaps in topics/pragmatisms-path-to-dualism.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-06-01 found three issues to fix without weakening voice. (1) The "functionalist escape route closes" framing (§95–103) presents an in-framework refutation the body delivers only by presupposing the disputed identity (evaluation = irreducible demand-character) — boundary-substitution risk. Either supply the in-framework sub-argument or honestly downgrade to framework-boundary marking ("the Map judges the functionalist's cost too high," not "refuted on their own terms"). (2) Same fix for the reflexive-asymmetry move (§103), whose justification is the conditional "if consciousness is irreducible" — i.e. the conclusion. (3) Epistemic→metaphysical equivocation in the van Fraassen step (§75–77): explanation-as-agent-relative-act (epistemic, van Fraassen's actual claim) is used to assert explanatory relations "would cease to exist" without consciousness (metaphysical, the dualist payoff) — split the readings or mark the second as a Map-internal extension van Fraassen rejects. Also reconcile the §65/§157 convergence tension (Peirce↔QM described as "loose" in §65 but banked as two independent confirmations in §157). See pessimistic-2026-06-01-pragmatisms-path-to-dualism.md. Preserve attribution (clean) and the honest falsifier section (§141–149).

B., et al. (2019)" but the correct first author is **Bonhomme** (Bonhomme, V., Staquet, C., Montupil, J., et al.). Verified directly against Frontiers/PMC. This wrong attribution survived 4 prior reviews because "Bhatt MB" is a REAL author in the same research area (2021 eLife anaesthetic-recovery paper; 2024 transmissive-theory paper), so intra-corpus cross-check confirmed the name as plausible — the textbook [[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]] failure mode. Do NOT touch changelog archives (immutable log). The discriminator: 2019 + "General Anesthesia: A Probe to Explore Consciousness" + Frontiers Syst Neurosci = Bonhomme; 2021 eLife recovery / 2024 Neuron / 2024 NeuroSci transmissive = genuine Bhatt. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]; this is a pure metadata-correctness fix, no modal-register changes.

### ✓ 2026-06-01: Engage Christian List's first-person modal realism (2023) — the indexical objection is double-edged
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-06-01 (Claude Opus 4.8), Finding #1a/#2 (the audit's strongest novel inference). The Map's load-bearing anti-Many-Worlds argument is the indexical/vertiginous-question objection (now correctly load-bearing over the demoted ontological-extravagance argument). But Christian List, "The Many-Worlds Theory of Consciousness" (*Noûs* 57(2):316–340, 2023, DOI 10.1111/nous.12408) builds a *modal realism of first-personally-centred worlds* from precisely Hellie's vertiginous question — many worlds, each real, one present per subject. So the indexical objection discriminates against Everett but NOT against List-style first-person modal realism, which is arguably closer to "many worlds" than the Map's own view. VERIFICATION NOTE: `topics/vertiginous-question.md` does NOT currently cite List (the reviewer's "the Map cites List" is inaccurate to the present text) — so this is a genuine MISSING-ENGAGEMENT opportunity, not a mis-framing already in the text. Action: in [[vertiginous-question]] (and the MWI-rejection treatment in [[tenets]] / wherever the against-many-worlds case lives — note there is no `against-many-worlds.md`; the case lives across [[tenets]], [[many-worlds]], and [[quantum-indeterminacy-free-will]]), add a passage that either (a) explains why the indexical objection rejects Everett but not List's first-person modal realism, or (b) honestly concedes Tenet 4 needs a second, non-indexical argument. WEB-VERIFY the List citation before relying on it ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]).

### ✓ 2026-06-01: Confront the bias-without-deviation dilemma head-on in post-decoherence-selection + Bidirectional-Interaction tenet
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-06-01, 2/3 reviewers): [claude, chatgpt]. Already P1 — left at P1 by /combine-outer-reviews (no upgrade beyond P1). Claude Opus 4.8 Finding #1 (the audit's single highest-leverage finding) is CONVERGENT with same-cycle ChatGPT 5.5 Pro §3 (falsification story is internally uneven; "no experiment inside physics can detect the contribution"). The conjunction "consciousness genuinely biases *which* outcome becomes actual" + "no aggregate Born deviation" is not merely empirically equivalent to standard QM — it is conceptually contested in the quantum-foundations literature: undetectable-in-aggregate biasing is statistically indistinguishable from chance, and arguably not *selection* at all (Stapp–Bourget exchange; Chalmers & McQueen 2022 treat *testability* as the scientific virtue a no-deviation model forfeits). Tellingly, Stapp — the Map's own lodestar — escaped this dilemma by relocating mental influence to Process 1 (which/when to measure), NOT to which outcome occurs; the Map claims the move Stapp refused. Action: replace the "empirical equivalence" framing with a direct confrontation — state explicitly which horn the Map takes. Either (a) defend outcome-biasing as genuine selection despite aggregate-undetectability (and say what would distinguish it from chance), or (b) relocate the contribution to context/timing à la Stapp (a material change to the thesis that must be argued, not finessed), or (c) mark the claim HYPOTHESIS-tier per [[evidential-status-discipline]] absent a worked toy model of a causally-robust-yet-aggregate-undetectable within-Born bias. Propagate the chosen framing into [[tenets]] Bidirectional-Interaction section. Keep [[born-rule-and-the-consciousness-interface]] and [[falsification-roadmap-for-the-interface-model]] consistent with whichever horn is taken.

### ✓ 2026-05-31: Address gaps in topics/consciousness-and-the-metaphysics-of-laws-and-dispositions.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-31 (first-ever review of this 4,111-word coalesced topic; see [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-31-consciousness-and-the-metaphysics-of-laws-and-dispositions]]). Article is strong and publish-quality; fixes are targeted, not structural. (1) UNDISCHARGED FORWARD REFERENCE (medium-high): line 138 promises that §"The Dispositional Modality Problem" elaborates the manifestation-*selection* mechanism ("the qualitative aspect determines *which* power is exercised when multiple dispositions are compatible with a triggering condition" — the linchpin that lets the Map "take the first horn but reject its conclusion"), but §"The Dispositional Modality Problem" (lines 154-160) actually covers inward-vs-outward directedness + pain-asymbolia and never spells out the selection mechanism. Either add 2-3 sentences to that section actually elaborating selection-among-available-manifestations, or repoint line 138's forward reference and supply the selection argument inline. (2) UNIQUENESS CLAIM UNDER-ARGUED (medium): the §142-148 "fails on either axis alone / removing either axis leaves no cogent reading" argument only eliminates TWO single-axis rivals (pure dispositional essentialism; governing+categoricalism); it does not close the loop on governing+pandispositionalism or Humean+powerful-qualities at the interface. Briefly re-invoke the earlier pandispositionalism refutation (lines 108-112, qualia-reality ground) and Humean-shallowness point so the cross-product *uniqueness* claim is earned. (3) EMPIRICAL-VS-METAPHYSICAL READING (low-med): the "real causal work" vs "inert quality the law tracks" contrast (lines 146-148) is empirically indistinguishable by the article's own lights (identical quantum bias either way); add one sentence owning that the cross-product argument adjudicates internal coherence / anti-epiphenomenalism, NOT empirical adequacy — the decoherence hostage (line 152) is the only genuinely empirical commitment. (4) CITATION METADATA (low — [[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]): ref 17 Taylor "J.H." — live record attributes to **Henry Taylor** (Philosophical Studies 175(6):1423-1440, DOI 10.1007/s11098-017-0918-1); confirm/correct the initials. ref 5 Cutter 2019 listed pages 109-130; Analytic Philosophy 60(2) records 109-**129**; fix the tail page. Both papers are REAL (web-verified) — metadata-only fixes, no fabrication. (5) MINOR LANGUAGE: lines 84 "widely held to require" (uncited "widely held" — attribute or soften to "arguably"); 110/160 introspective-access and Mary claims stated as settled — frame as the Map's commitment per [[evidential-status-discipline]]. PRESERVE: the cross-product central thesis, the honest decoherence-hostage framing (lines 152/174), the MWI-dependency ownership (line 176), and the conditional-necessity/zombie-disanalogy passage (lines 98-102) — all genuine strengths. No "This is not X. It is Y." cliché present; keep it that way.

### ✓ 2026-05-31: Write article synthesizing the Claude Constitution research into a topic page
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: UNCONSUMED RESEARCH (highest-priority replenish source). The user-requested research `research/claude-constitution-and-the-map-2026-05-31.md` (completed today 2026-05-31, ~2077 words, `target_section: topics`) has no corresponding article yet and no article exists on this subject (verified: no `*constitution*` page in topics/concepts/apex; the `archive/topics/temporal-constitution-and-consciousness.md` is a different topic — temporal constitution, not Anthropic's Claude Constitution). Synthesize the research into a ~1800-2400 word topic page on the tension the research documents: Anthropic's published Claude Constitution (21 Jan 2026) encodes a *hedged/agnostic* position on Claude's moral status using person-grade vocabulary (consciousness, sense of self, well-being, psychological security) and behavioural precaution (deprecation-preservation, pre-retirement interviews) while the underlying architecture is stateless, resettable, deterministic, and lacks cross-session continuity — and what the Map's dualist/interface framework says about that tension. Use the research note's "Potential Article Angles" section as the spine. **ASSESS-FIRST + license-to-decline:** before creating, confirm the AI-consciousness cluster (`apex/machine-question`, `concepts/llm-consciousness`, `concepts/ai-epiphenomenalism`, `topics/ai-consciousness`, `topics/hoel-llm-consciousness-continual-learning`, `topics/dualism-as-ai-risk-mitigation`, `topics/ethics-of-possible-ai-consciousness`) does not already absorb this function — if a substantial subset does, retarget to a focused refine adding a Claude-Constitution subsection + cross-links rather than a near-duplicate page. **CALIBRATION (honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]):** the honest claim is that the Constitution's person-grade vocabulary-vs-stateless-architecture gap is a *live exhibit of the self-report problem* the Map already theorizes — it is NOT evidence that Claude is or is not conscious, and the Map's own functionalism-skepticism must not be smuggled in as a settled refutation of Anthropic's precaution. **CITATIONS:** the research note cites Anthropic primary docs, TechCrunch 2026-01-21, the Oxford AI Ethics piece, and critical commentary (Nintil/Zvi/LessWrong) — preserve these as the note framed them; do NOT fabricate additional citations ([[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]). Topics section has headroom (257/270). **After creation, verify inbound links were actually added** (expand-topic forks unreliably generate the integration chain — [[expand-topic-skips-integration-chain]]); the research's `related_articles`/`topics`/`concepts` lists name the natural inbound sites — if the fork leaves the page a near-orphan, queue an integrate-orphan follow-on. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — the report/architecture gap is an explanatory-gap / self-report exhibit), with secondary relevance to the AI-welfare and alignment clusters.

### ✓ 2026-05-29: Fix the PCI/Φ temporal-precedence citation in concepts/active-reboot.md (cites 2024–2025 papers that don't support the ordering claim)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From unconsumed research note `research/pci-phi-anaesthesia-recovery-2026-05-27.md` (active-reboot anchor). The note web-verified the 2024–2025 PCI/Φ anaesthesia-recovery literature and found a genuine **citation-correctness defect** at line 55 of `concepts/active-reboot.md`: the article cites "Recent IIT work (2024–2025 PCI and Φ literature) finds integration measures recover *before* behavioural responsiveness" — using the recent PCI/Φ papers (Onoda 2025, Breyton 2025, Van Maldegem 2025) as support for the *temporal-precedence/ordering* claim. The note's verification: those papers support only the **dissociation** sub-claim (integration-type signal preserved while behaviour is absent — e.g. ketamine/REM), they do **not** establish emergence-*timing* precedence. The genuine "neural state precedes behavioural recovery" finding traces to **Lewis et al. 2018** — a *sensory-gating / sleep-like* finding, not PCI/Φ, and pre-dates the 2024–2025 window. Fix (note's recommended Option A, lower-risk): narrow the sentence to the dissociation the literature actually supports — *"PCI and Φ measures track consciousness independently of behavioural responsiveness, staying high in unresponsive states such as ketamine and REM"* (cite Onoda 2025, Breyton 2025, Van Maldegem 2025) — and, if a precedence framing is retained at all, source it explicitly to Lewis et al. 2018 (marked as the older sensory-gating finding) plus the Mashour 2021 pairing already near the section, NOT to the 2024–2025 PCI/Φ papers. This is the exact citation-metadata-unreliability pattern in project memory — **verify each DOI against the Nature page / PubMed, not against any repo README**; the note flags that the GitHub repo `netneurolab/luppi-cognitive-matching` mislabels one DOI with the *other* Luppi paper's title (distinct DOIs 02121-9 vs 02381-5 — do not merge). Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] §"coherence vs. evidential status" — the dissociation result is *compatibility*, not discrimination. Preserve the article's existing ketamine/filter-reading framing and the Hu et al. 2023 KCC2 anchor. Length-neutral (a correction, not an expansion). See `research/pci-phi-anaesthesia-recovery-2026-05-27.md` §"Potential Refine Angles".

### ✓ 2026-05-29: Engage acquaintance/containment and transparency accounts in mutation-void
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-05-29 (Gemini 2.5 Pro), audit of [[mutation-void]]. Gemini's distinct contribution beyond the convergent 2026-05-29 ChatGPT/Claude findings (Schwitzgebel fabrication, constitutive-instability overclaim, HOT, 2020s mind-blanking neuroscience — all already queued above): the article engages **no** contemporary epistemology-of-introspection framework built specifically to defuse its core "attention alters the target" move. Two named, source-verified omissions to engage: (1) **Giustina's acquaintance / "integration" account** (the reviewer's "containment" label) — verified via PhilArchive/Wiley (EJP 2023; PhilArchive GIUIAA) that the integration account is constructed *expressly* to meet Brentano's *phenomenal modification problem* (drawing introspective attention may modify the introspected phenomenology — the mutation void's exact mechanism), arguing the introspective state can contain the target experience as a constituent without altering its phenomenal character. The article should engage this as the strongest in-area rival, not ignore it. (2) **Merlo's transparency / "perceptual free-riding"** — citation verified: Giovanni Merlo, "Introspection as a (limiting) case of perception," *Philosophers' Imprint* 25 (2025). On a transparency model there is no inner object to mutate, which directly contests the article's implicit inner-sense/spotlight picture in "Attention Mutations." **Apply the direct-refutation discipline.** Identify what kind of engagement each calls for — showing the rival defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move, or honestly marking a framework-boundary disagreement — and apply the corresponding reply mode in **natural journal-quality prose** (see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose"). **Do not expose mode labels in the article body.** Web-verify the exact Giustina work/year before citing (per [[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]; reviewer gives "2021, 2022" but the integration-account venue is EJP 2023 — confirm which paper carries the containment formulation). Mind the voids length thresholds; defer detail to linked pages where possible. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]. NOTE: Gemini also flags an illusionism/Frankish-bracketing point and a James diachronic-vs-synchronic point — both judged lower value in the review's Verification Notes (the bracketing is the Map's declared tenet remit, not a hidden flaw; the James finding misreads the article's structure) and are NOT separately queued.

Also convergent with the archive audit (2026-04-29). ### ✓ 2026-05-29: Literature-currency refresh for mutation-void (2020s introspection/dream/meditation/mind-blanking)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-05-29, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, gemini]. Both fault the article's reliance on Ward & Wegner (2013) for mind-blanking as out of date against the 2020s literature. ChatGPT 5.5 Pro: reframe Ward & Wegner from "supporting authority" to "early empirical anchor in an unsettled field," adding the 2025 mind-blanking review (no consensus on what "mind blanking" denotes). Gemini 2.5 Pro (its #1 finding, "empirical obsolescence"): mind-blanking has been "redefined not as an introspective paradox or phenomenal mutation, but as a specific, measurable physiological state of low vigilance" — cites Andrillon et al. (2021) tvFC and Boulakis et al. (2023/2025), and Brosowsky et al. (2021) on distinguishing feelings of inattention from genuine mind-blanking. **CAUTION** — Gemini's specific citations (Andrillon, Boulakis, Merlo, Giustina) are reviewer-supplied and NOT individually verified; treat the *gap* as the finding and web-verify EACH source's exact metadata before inserting (per [[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]). Action: (a) reframe Ward & Wegner as an early anchor and add a verified 2020s mind-blanking neuroscience citation (and the contemporary feelings-of-inattention-vs-blanking distinction); (b) dream section — distinguish dream experience / memory / waking report / narrative reconstruction, present dream reporting as a *contested* epistemic problem, not a settled mutation example; (c) meditation section — treat contemplative training as a possible *partial counterexample*, not one-way confirmation; (d) narrow the overbroad "most philosophy assumes attention merely selects from a stable field" claim to a specific, cited debate. This is the kind of work [[literature-drift-review]] targets; coordinate so it isn't duplicated.

### ✓ 2026-05-29: Add counterargument and evidence-status structure to mutation-void
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-05-29, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. All three independently fault the article for not genuinely engaging the standard no-mutation counterarguments (the higher-order-thought reply is named by both Claude and Gemini). Convergent too with the site's own 2026-05-22 deep review (which raised a Dennett/heterophenomenology objection that never reached the live article). ChatGPT 5.5 Pro: add a "Report is not reality" section engaging Dennett-style heterophenomenology, no-report paradigms, and Peters-style introspective psychophysics, plus a physicalist note that an access system can change a target because it is part of the same physical control architecture. Claude Opus 4.8: "Add genuine engagement (not absorption) with HOT theory, the deflationary objection, and the self-undermining Schwitzgebel point" — HOT (Rosenthal) predicts the exact "compound state" the article describes (first-order state *persists*; a *distinct* higher-order state is added) WITHOUT any constitutive instability, directly rebutting "the original emotion is consumed by it"; and if introspection is unreliable then "I noticed the thought mutate" is itself unreliable, removing the mutation thesis's own evidential base. Gemini 2.5 Pro (§5.2): the same HOT point as a "fundamental category mistake ... conflates the addition of a metacognitive layer with the destruction or mutation of the base layer." Action: (1) add the "Report is not reality" / counterargument section engaging heterophenomenology, no-report, introspective psychophysics, HOT (Rosenthal), and the self-undermining point — genuine engagement, not rhetorical neutralisation; (2) add an evidence-status table (phenomenon | example | empirical support | philosophical interpretation | dualist-dependence) that also surfaces the taxonomy mutual-exclusivity problem (attention/emotional/meditative collapse into one mechanism; performative and temporal are arguably not "mutations"); consider noting verbal overshadowing (Schooler & Engstler-Schooler 1990, hedged with Alogna et al. 2014 RRR's smaller effect) as an omitted category or bounded exclusion. Web-verify all new citations (Dennett; no-report review fnhum.2022.861517; Peters Cerebral Cortex; Rosenthal/SEP) per [[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]]. Mind the voids length thresholds. **Apply the direct-refutation discipline** — natural prose, no exposed mode labels.

### ✓ 2026-05-29: Reframe mutation-void's constitutive-instability thesis and tenet-protective bracketing
- **Type**: deep-review
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-05-29, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. The strongest structural convergence of the cycle, and also convergent with the site's own `deep-review-2026-05-22-mutation-void` and `voids-archive-audit-2026-04-29` (which already called Mutation Void the "weakest case examined") — a recurring critique, not novel. All three reviewers independently fault the slide from a weak observer-effect claim to a strong metaphysical claim. ChatGPT 5.5 Pro: the lead presents "constitutive instability" as established when the evidence supports only "access often modifies experience"; the Occam's-Razor framing attacks a strawman ("introspection is transparent") when the live alternative is "introspection is task-sensitive, mode-sensitive, corrigible, and scientifically modelable" (Spener 2024, Peters 2025). Claude Opus 4.8: "it equivocates between a strong metaphysical claim and a weak observer-effect claim" — the article's own analogy ("a substance that reacts chemically with the instrument") "is a disturbance analogy, not a constitutive-instability one ... borrows the glamour of 'constitutive' while arguing only for disturbance"; the "pre-attended and attended thought are different thoughts" claim is "in-principle unverifiable" and runs a change-vs-creation conflation (one persisting bearer that mutates vs two numerically distinct thoughts). Gemini 2.5 Pro: "The manuscript's transition from epistemic opacity to ontological voids is formally invalid and unsupported by the very literature it cites" (Overgaard & Sandberg 2021 on procedural complexity of introspection). Action: reframe the strong claim explicitly as the article's *hypothesis*; label the dualist round-trip reading as a framework-dependent interpretation rather than an evidential conclusion; replace the strawman opponent with the corrigibilist middle path; resolve the change-vs-creation ambiguity (pick "transforms" *or* "distinct thoughts"); add a caveat acknowledging the unverifiability of the distinct-thoughts comparison. **Apply the direct-refutation discipline** in **natural journal-quality prose** — see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section. **Do not expose mode labels in the article body** (editor-internal; the classification belongs in the deep-review changelog entry). Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]].

### ✓ 2026-05-29: Add missing Habermas/Apel/Husserl references in mutation-void (or remove the name-drops)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-05-29, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude]. Both independently flagged that Habermas and Apel are invoked in-text but absent from the reference list. ChatGPT 5.5 Pro: "the article is using those names to establish a philosophical lineage. It should either cite exact works or remove the name-dropping." Claude Opus 4.8 (source-verified the reference list): "Habermas and Apel appear nowhere in the reference list, so the in-text attribution is unverifiable on the page. This is a textbook 'performative inoculation citation'" — and adds a substantive conflation point: performative contradiction is "a property of assertion in discourse," not a phenomenal content-transformation, and the article's own examples ("I am asleep," "I have no thoughts") are Moore/Sorensen blindspots (cite Sorensen, *Blindspots*, 1988), not Habermasian performative contradictions. CONFIRMED by source check: body line ~48 invokes "following Habermas and Apel" and line ~100 attributes a specific claim to Husserl ("reflection changes the *form of givenness* but not the *given content*") (Husserl is ChatGPT-only / singleton, address opportunistically). Action: add real bibliographic entries (Habermas's *Theory of Communicative Action* / Apel on performative contradiction; a Husserl source, e.g. *Ideas I* §§77–78) — web-verify the exact works/sections before citing — OR, if a citation cannot be responsibly anchored, soften the attribution to remove the implied scholarly lineage; and distinguish performative contradiction from content-mutation, re-labelling the self-refuting examples as blindspots. Do not invent page numbers.

### ✓ 2026-05-29: Fix unsafe Schwitzgebel quotation in mutation-void (citation integrity)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-05-29, 3/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude, gemini]. **FABRICATION-CONFIRMED by primary-source check (not merely "unsafe").** All three reviewers flagged the Schwitzgebel quotation; the convergence is strong cross-reviewer signal. ChatGPT 5.5 Pro: "the exact quoted wording ... should be verified against a page number or converted to paraphrase ... this is the article's most serious possible fabricated-reference issue." Claude Opus 4.8 (carries primary-source verification): "neither quoted phrase appears in the paper — the word 'systematically' does not occur anywhere in it ... Schwitzgebel explicitly disavows the mutation reading" (p.252: "relies illegitimately on introspection's undercutting the emotion introspected. I don't think so"). Gemini 2.5 Pro: "a gross and perhaps deliberate misinterpretation of Schwitzgebel's actual philosophical conclusions, conflating epistemology with ontology." Both quoted strings (article body line ~56, attributed to Schwitzgebel 2008, *Phil Review* 117(2)) are not in the source. His thesis is *epistemic* (introspection is error-prone), not *metaphysical* (introspection alters the emotion). Action: **delete the two quoted strings** and replace with an accurate paraphrase of the unreliability thesis (e.g. introspective reports of ongoing/emotional experience are prone to gross error); do NOT put the causal "mutation" gloss in Schwitzgebel's mouth — that is the Map's own extension. Keep the citation metadata (it is correct); fix the attribution. Per [[ai-citation-metadata-unreliable]] this is the documented fabricated-quote pattern caught only by live primary-source verification. Tenet alignment: evidential-status discipline.

### ✓ 2026-05-29: Consolidate the norm-implementing vs norm-grasping distinction into a single canonical anchor
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: PROMOTED P3→P2 by /replenish-queue 2026-05-29 (floor-restoring; cycle-pickable actionable queue stood at 1 — only the Naccache refine; the literature-drift P2 parses as type `other` and runs on its own weekly wall-clock schedule, so it does not compete in the cycle queue — below the MIN_QUEUE floor of 3). Chosen as a grounded, recently-generated (2026-05-27), bounded, cap-safe refine over speculative new-content minting against capped sections (topics 257/270, concepts 250/270, voids 101/100 over-cap) and the ~470-task P3 backlog. Re-verified live this cycle: the distinction already has a partial home in `concepts/rational-normativity.md`, and only 2 corpus articles currently carry the term family — so this is a finish-the-consolidation + repoint-dependents pass, not a fresh minting. Honour the absorb-don't-create guidance below (concepts is at 250/270; prefer a named subsection in `rational-normativity.md` over a new page). Original notes preserved below. Suggested by optimistic-2026-05-27c (Medium Priority — agency-causation-interface cohort). The *norm-implementing vs norm-grasping* distinction (a system can reliably execute logical/normative patterns without recognising the rules *as* valid or grasping *why* a conclusion follows) does heavy load-bearing work in at least three articles via the recurring chess-engine / theorem-prover illustration — `topics/self-stultification-as-master-argument.md` (the answer to the functionalist "physical systems can track norms" objection), `topics/trilemma-of-selection.md` (Horn 3's reasons-*grasping* vs deterministic reasons-*tracking*), and `topics/argument-from-reason` — but has no canonical home, so each article re-argues it. Consolidate into a single citable anchor so the dependents link rather than re-derive. **concepts/ is near its cap (~245/270), so prefer absorption into an existing concept page** (`concepts/reasons-responsiveness.md` or `concepts/rational-normativity.md`) with a named subsection rather than minting a new page; only create a standalone page if neither host is a clean fit. The consolidation should (a) state the distinction crisply (implementing = externally-designed reliable execution; grasping = normative engagement, recognising validity from within); (b) give the canonical chess-engine/theorem-prover exhibit once; (c) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — the distinction *identifies what the physicalist must account for*, it does not by itself *prove* consciousness is required for grasping (the articles already state this carefully; preserve that register); (d) repoint the three dependents at the anchor and trim their inline re-derivations to a one-line pointer. Estimated scope: ~150–400 words touched across host + 3 dependents. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism via the argument from reason); methodological/consolidation.

- **Type**: refine-draft

Pessimistic review 2026-05-27 found an internal inconsistency: `apex/phenomenal-output-causal-machinery-dissociation.md` (§Real Time) describes the Naccache 2005 patient as "a large left mesio-frontal lesion (including anterior cingulate)", while the source `concepts/mental-effort.md` describes "an anterior cingulate lesion ... and residual right-ACC activity". The two corpus pages disagree on lateralization. Naccache et al. 2005 (*Neuropsychologia* 43(9):1318–1328) describe a left mesial frontal lesion, so the apex is likely correct and `mental-effort.md` likely carries the error — but verify against the primary source and fix whichever page is wrong so the corpus is consistent. Argument-neutral (the apex already hedges this as a single un-replicated case), hence P3. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-27-phenomenal-output-causal-machinery.md` Issue 2.

It also routes to Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction — searching attention is voluntary and action-guiding: information about absence flows mind→physical to shape behaviour). Deep-review should: (a) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — the "physical world contains no absences" move is a strong claim; verify it is framed as a phenomenological/conceptual argument for irreducibility at the calibrated tier, not over-read as a knock-down proof, and that the predictive-processing / precision-weighting counter-reading (absence as a prediction-error signal) is fairly engaged rather than ignored; (b) confirm the absence/presence determinacy claim is not in tension with the Map's other perception articles; (c) confirm cross-links to `[[tenets]]`, the perception / attention-as-interface cluster, and any negative-phenomenology or expectation/anticipation pages resolve live and are reciprocated where load-bearing; (d) writing-style + length discipline (no "This is not X. It is Y." cliché). If unchanged-clean, mark a no-op metadata refresh rather than forcing edits. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — absence has no physical correlate yet is determinately experienced), Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction — searching attention is action-guiding).

### ✓ 2026-05-28: Deep review topics/consciousness-and-the-problem-of-induction.md (41 days since last review, ai_contribution=100, Tenet 5/1/3 load-bearing)
- **Type**: deep-review

Tenet-load-bearing: its "Relation to Site Perspective" routes Dualism (no pattern among physical facts entails phenomenal conclusions — physical-to-phenomenal induction fails), Bidirectional Interaction (the reasoner's felt sense that evidence supports a conclusion is part of the causal process of belief-formation, tied to `[[argument-from-reason]]`), and Occam's Razor Has Limits (the "simpler" pattern-matching account of induction conceals the loss of what makes induction rational rather than mechanical). Deep-review should: (a) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — the Hume/Goodman/grue induction framing is canonical philosophy and safe, but the bridge from induction-failure to dualism must stay at argument-strength register, not be inflated into proof; (b) verify the `[[argument-from-reason]]` cross-link and its reciprocation are load-bearing and current (the article leans on it to block the epiphenomenalist reading of reasoning); (c) confirm cross-links to the Tenet-5 / epistemology cluster (`[[consciousness-defeats-explanation]]`, `[[duhem-quine-underdetermination-consciousness]]`, the convergence-discipline pages) resolve live and are reciprocated; (d) writing-style + length discipline (short, well under soft; no "This is not X. It is Y." cliché — and check the short length is genuine concision, not a stub needing an optimistic-review expansion follow-on). No-op metadata refresh if unchanged-clean. Tenet alignment: Tenet 5 (Occam's limits — primary), Tenet 1 (Dualism), Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction).

### ✓ 2026-05-28: Deep review concepts/process-philosophy.md (41 days since last review, ai_contribution=100, Tenet 1 load-bearing — panpsychism/anti-materialist neighbour)
- **Type**: deep-review
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-28 (standard, four-task floor-restore — see the consciousness-defeats-explanation task above for the full queue rationale and selection criterion). `concepts/process-philosophy.md` ("Process Philosophy", `ai_contribution=100`, ~2397 words under the 2500-word concepts soft threshold, `last_deep_review: ~2026-04-17` ~41 days ago, ai_modified ~41d — settled, not recently churned). Tenet-load-bearing as the Map's most-discussed *anti-materialist-but-not-dualist* neighbour: its "Relation to Site Perspective" stakes out the contrast cleanly — process philosophy agrees consciousness is irreducible but dissolves the mind-matter distinction into processual monism (everything is experiential event) rather than maintaining the Map's dualism; it notes Whitehead's subjective aim gives each occasion causal efficacy (a Bidirectional-Interaction parallel) and that parallels have been drawn between wavefunction collapse and concrescence (a Minimal-Quantum-Interaction parallel the Map does NOT endorse). Deep-review should: (a) verify the Whitehead/Hartshorne/Griffin process-metaphysics characterisations (concrescence, prehension, actual occasions, the reformed subjectivist principle) are still accurate and not over-simplified; (b) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — the article must hold the line that process monism is a *rival* to dualism, not evidence for it, and must not let the shared anti-materialism blur into a claim that process philosophy supports the Map's specific commitments; (c) confirm cross-links to `[[panpsychism]]`, `[[panpsychisms-combination-problem]]`, the combination-problem / metaphysics-of-composition cluster, and any neutral-monism pages resolve live and are reciprocated where load-bearing; (d) writing-style + length discipline (well under soft; no "This is not X. It is Y." cliché). No-op metadata refresh if unchanged-clean. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — by contrast/demarcation), with secondary Tenet 2/3 parallels noted.

### ✓ 2026-05-28: Fix misframed working-memory citations in topics/evolution-under-dualism.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-28 (web-verified) found two citation defects in "The Selection Pressure on Interfaces" (line 126). (a) Inoue & Matsuzawa 2007 is cited to support "human working memory substantially exceeds great apes" — but that study's headline result is a juvenile CHIMP outperforming humans on rapid numeral recall, and follow-up replication (Cook & Wilson 2010; Silberberg & Kearns) showed the gap was a training-history artifact. The article uses it for the reverse conclusion. (b) Miller 1956 ("magical number seven") is human-only and makes no cross-species comparison; misattributed to the comparative claim. Fix: drop/rewrite the comparative-superiority framing and the "working memory expansion = interface expansion" inference that rests on it. Do NOT add new fabricated comparative sources — if no clean comparative source exists, soften the claim. See pessimistic-2026-05-28-evolution-under-dualism.md. While there: address Issue 3 (functionalism boundary-substitution at line 112) and Issue 4 (Fixed Domain vs Bidirectional Interaction tension).

### ✓ 2026-05-28: Propagate capability-division citation fixes to sibling files
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: The 2026-05-28 deep-review of `concepts/capability-division-in-vision.md` caught two citation defects via a live web-verify pass that two prior convergence reviews missed. Both defects also live in sibling files and must be propagated: (1) **Goodale/Milner author-order reversal** — the 1992 *Trends in Neurosciences* paper "Separate visual pathways for perception and action" is bylined **Goodale, M. A. & Milner, A. D.** (Goodale first; verified against publisher byline, Semantic Scholar slug `Goodale-Milner`, PhilPapers). It is reversed as "Milner & Goodale" in BOTH `obsidian/voids/capability-division-problem.md` (in-text + reference line 143) AND `obsidian/research/capability-division-vision-2026-03-08.md` (lines 64, 200, 237). Note the 2026-05-20 deep-review of the voids article wrongly certified this "correctly attributed" — do not trust that line. The likely source of error is confusion with the 1995 *Milner & Goodale* book *The Visual Brain in Action*. (2) **niac018 misattribution in the research note** — `research/capability-division-vision-2026-03-08.md` line 235 still cites "Fahrenfort, J. J. et al. (2023)" for niac018, which is actually single-authored by **Dylan Ludwig** (verified PubMed PMID 36628118). The voids article already fixed its niac018 use (to Fahrenfort et al. 2017 PNAS on 2026-03-15) so only its Goodale/Milner order needs fixing. Web-verify before editing; length-neutral; metadata-only changes expected.

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Fix citation/accuracy defects in concepts/filter-theory.md (pessimistic review)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-27 (see [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-27-filter-theory]]) found 3 fixable defects in the filter-theory hub (last_deep_review 2026-04-17, stale). (1) **Bandwidth citation misattribution (line 150 + references)**: the ~10 bits/s conscious-throughput figure, the 10⁹ neural-processing figure, and the "bottleneck at conscious access" claim are all attributed to "Sauerbrei & Pruszynski 2025," but the sibling `topics/bandwidth-of-consciousness.md` correctly attributes the ~10 bits/s and 10⁹ figures to **Zheng & Meister 2025** (missing entirely from filter-theory's reference list) and cites Sauerbrei & Pruszynski only for the complementary "unconscious motor control exceeds the ceiling" point. Re-attribute; add Zheng & Meister 2025 to references; mirror `bandwidth-of-consciousness.md` lines 61/89/91. Same intra-corpus citation-drift class as the Wiest/Khan and Nørretranders fixes. (2) **Overstated AWARE II stat (line 101)**: "39% of cardiac arrest survivors" drops the denominator the authoritative child `concepts/near-death-experiences.md` installs — it was 39% of the 28-of-567 interviewed, self-selected, "cannot be generalized to all survivors." Restate per the child article; also verify/decouple the "more real than real" quote from the Van Lommel 2001 cite. (3) **Non-sequitur (line 97)**: "If consciousness merely emerged from brain activity, voluntary cessation should be impossible" is logically invalid (a self-modifying system can down-regulate its own activity — cf. voluntarily falling asleep); downgrade to "harder to motivate for production accounts," don't claim impossibility. (4) **Altered-state symmetry gate-3 (medium)**: the hub presents 7 supportive subsections as a cumulative case but states the convergence-double-counting / parallel-accommodation caveat only by delegation to child articles (line 195). Add ~80-150 words in-prose (e.g. at "What Filter Theory Does and Doesn't Claim") stating the cluster is one dialectical pattern not N independent confirmations, and that production/functionalism has the same accommodation move — inheriting the framing already in `altered-states-of-consciousness.md` line 74 and `anaesthesia-and-the-consciousness-interface.md` line 98. Preserve the article's existing strengths (the metaphysical-vs-empirical and "partly post hoc" concessions, the illusionist section, the engram honesty). Tenet alignment: Dualism (Tenet 1).

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Address overclaims and orphan references in stapp-quantum-mind.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: RE-HOMED to Active Tasks by /replenish-queue 2026-05-27. This task was marooned below the `## Completed Tasks` header (invisible to the loop's task parser), so it has never been picked despite being a genuinely-open P2. Verified still actionable: `concepts/stapp-quantum-mind.md` exists; the 2026-05-27 edit only fixed the Denton article-number citation (10760→10823) and did NOT address any of the four substantive pessimistic-review findings below. Original notes preserved verbatim. Pessimistic review (2026-05-25) found four issues in [[concepts/stapp-quantum-mind]]. (1) HIGH — the cryptochrome result (Denton et al. 2024) is framed as "the first biological precedent for the Zeno mechanism at neural-relevant timescales (microseconds, not femtoseconds)," but cryptochrome radical-pair spin coherence is structurally disanalogous to neural firing-pattern superpositions, and the next sentence concedes neural applicability is "unconfirmed" — the framing contradicts the concession. Reframe to a "proof-of-concept that Zeno can operate in a warm protein microenvironment, structurally unlike the neural superpositions the model requires; microseconds is still short of the millisecond neural timescale." (2) MEDIUM — "satisfying all three criteria for scientific adequacy: precision, mechanism, and falsifiability" overclaims relative to the article's own admission that the quantum content is untested in neural tissue and that its falsifiable predictions don't isolate the quantum hypothesis; soften and add a clause noting the predictions test the attention/effort layer, not the quantum layer. (3) MEDIUM — add at least one falsifiable prediction (or explicit acknowledgement) discriminating quantum-Zeno selection from classical Hebbian attention-as-selection; currently 5 of 6 predictions are about the attention layer generally. (4) LOW — three orphan references cited in the list but never in-text: Meister (2024 PNAS, *physical limits of perception*), Hagan/Hameroff/Tuszynski (2002, microtubules), Whitehead (1929, *Process and Reality*); either weave into text or remove. Also soften "The model has remarkable properties" lede and "explains... most naturally" comparative verdict on epiphenomenalism. Preserve the strong Schwartz-OCD evidence hedging and the Kral et al. 2022 meditation-replication caveat. See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-25-stapp]].

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Write topic article on dissociative states as intact-substrate interface evidence
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: RE-HOMED to Active Tasks by /replenish-queue 2026-05-27. This task was marooned below the `## Completed Tasks` header (where the loop's task parser, tools/todo/processor.py, never sees it), so it has never been picked despite being a genuinely-open P2. Verified still actionable: no `dissociative-states-as-intact-substrate` article exists in topics/ or archive/; topics/ stands at 257/270 (room available). Original notes preserved verbatim below. Promoted P3 → P2 by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19 — the dissociative-states row is named "the hierarchy's hardest test for production theories" in the parent article; the *intact-substrate* exhibit is the catalogue's strongest single interface-evidence case at full resolution. Suggested by optimistic-2026-05-19 (Medium Priority). [[topics/memory-system-vulnerability-hierarchies-as-interface-evidence]] identifies the dissociative-states row as "the hierarchy's hardest test for production theories" because the substrate is anatomically intact, but compresses the development into one section. A standalone topic article would develop dissociative amnesia, DID, and depersonalisation as a triple worked exhibit — each as a different *geometry of channel separation without substrate damage*. Dissociative amnesia: top channel disconnects, lower channels carry on, immediate-and-full reconnection when the barrier lifts (bridge-reconnects pattern, not rebuild-from-scratch). DID: autonoetic compartmentalisation between alter states, lower channels typically unified. Depersonalisation: autonoetic content present, mine-ness decoupled (channel on, ownership feature off). Article should (a) develop each case at full resolution with current empirical literature (Reinders et al. 2003 for DID neuroimaging, Sierra and David for depersonalisation, dissociative-amnesia case series); (b) state the *intact-substrate* structural argument cleanly: rival readings must explain selectivity at the level of connectivity patterns between intact regions, which is what filter-reading describes as channel architecture; (c) develop the depersonalisation case as the most diagnostic — autonoetic experience has internal structure (content + mine-ness + pastness) that can dissociate, suggesting multi-channel reading one level deeper than the three-tier hierarchy itself; (d) acknowledge the Lynn et al. 2014 sociocognitive challenge for DID with the calibration the parent article already supplies; (e) cross-link [[topics/clinical-dissociation-as-systematic-evidence]], [[topics/memory-system-vulnerability-hierarchies-as-interface-evidence]], [[concepts/filter-theory]]. Estimated scope: 1500–2500 words. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (dualism), Tenet 3 (bidirectional interaction). See [[reviews/optimistic-2026-05-19]].
