### ✓ 2026-05-27: Downgrade the "fifth option" taxonomic claim and properly credit Rawlette in topics/meaning-of-life.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-05-27 (Claude Opus 4.7), Axis 1 + §4.5 — the reviewer's central structural finding, **verified against the live article**. (1) **The "fifth option" claim (line 73) over-claims.** On Metz's taxonomy the position is not outside the four-way split: it is either a phenomenologically-refined *subjective* naturalism (if phenomenal significance gets its normative force from being someone's felt valence/pro-attitude) or *objective* naturalism with a phenomenal-property value-bearer (if its force is stance-independent) — the article never crisply answers which. The companion [[phenomenal-value-realism]] article *concedes* the view "can be held within physicalist frameworks — Rawlette herself does not commit to dualism," which is in tension with a strong taxonomic-novelty claim. Replace "fifth option" with the more defensible framing: a phenomenal-value *implementation* of objective naturalism distinguished at the level of the value-*bearer*, OR — more defensibly still — "an experientialist account of meaning that Metz's taxonomy excludes *by stipulation* (the experience-machine intuition) rather than refutes." Distinctiveness is dualism's ontology, not a new taxonomic category. (2) **Credit Rawlette as the source.** The core "fifth option" *is* Rawlette's metaethical identity move (*The Feeling of Value*, 2016 — phenomenal pleasantness *constitutes* goodness via analytic descriptivism) extended to meaning; the article currently uses her as one supporting citation (line 83). Open "The Map's Position" by crediting Rawlette explicitly, then state the **distinguishing move** (Rawlette is a hedonistic monist; the Map extends to phenomenal-value *pluralism* and grounds it in dualism rather than physicalism — she would not necessarily endorse this). Mirror the credit pattern already used in [[phenomenal-value-realism]]. Note honestly that Rawlette's identity claim is contested / lightly engaged in mainstream metaethics, rather than presenting it as settled territory. **Apply the direct-refutation discipline** ([[direct-refutation-discipline]]): this is a boundary-substitution / unearned-foundational-move risk — the taxonomic-novelty claim is not earned by the framework's own standards, and the identity premise (phenomenal character = intrinsic value) is assumed where the nihilist denies it. Mark the dependency honestly per [[evidential-status-discipline]]; write in **natural journal-quality prose** ([[writing-style]] "Engaging Opponents"); **no mode labels in the body**. CONVERGENT (over-claiming theme, 2/3 reviewers): this is the *taxonomic*-over-claim half of the cluster ChatGPT also flagged — ChatGPT 5.5 Pro independently judged "the meaning article often writes as if rival views have been defeated rather than as if the conclusion is tenet-relative," and its sibling *quantum*-over-claim is the P1 "evidential-status / tenet-dependency labelling" task. The two together calibrate the article to its own honesty — coordinate so the fifth-option re-framing and the dependency box land coherently. (Already P1; not upgraded further.) See [[reviews/outer-review-2026-05-27-claude-opus-4-7]] and [[reviews/outer-review-2026-05-27-chatgpt-5-5-pro]].

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Fix named-philosopher misfilings in topics/meaning-of-life.md (Benatar, Landau, Nagel)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-05-27 (Claude Opus 4.7), Axis 3 — the reviewer's "most damaging fairness problems," all **verified against the live article** (see Verification Notes in the review file). (1) **Benatar misclassified.** Line 69 files Benatar under the `### Nihilism` heading with the gloss "existence always involves more harm than non-existence" — that is the *Better Never to Have Been* (2006) anti-natalism argument (the only Benatar work in References, line 281), not his meaning view. In *The Human Predicament* (2017) Benatar **grants terrestrial meaning while denying cosmic meaning** — a terrestrial-yes/cosmic-no hybrid that is closer to the Map's own position than the article admits, yet reaches the opposite affective conclusion. Give Benatar his own paragraph as the cosmic-pessimist, cite *The Human Predicament* (2017), and engage the actual cosmic-meaning-asymmetry argument. (2) **Landau misfiled.** Line 69 places Landau under `### Nihilism`, but his text ("the implications of cosmic meaninglessness are overstated") describes a *defender* of meaning. Move him out of the nihilist section entirely — he is a fellow defender of meaning under cosmic insignificance (*Finding Meaning in an Imperfect World*, OUP 2017). (3) **Nagel thin/off.** Line 69 undersells Nagel: "The Absurd" (1971) explicitly *rejects* the cosmic-smallness reading and locates absurdity in a structural feature of reflective consciousness — so Nagel is closer to the Map than to nihilism. State this; note immortality/scale don't change his analysis. **Apply the direct-refutation discipline** ([[direct-refutation-discipline]]): the core issue is that the *named position is not the actual position* (strawman/misfiling) — fix by accurately characterising the strongest version of each position before any criticism, in **natural journal-quality prose** per [[writing-style]]'s "Engaging Opponents" section; **no mode labels in the body** (changelog only). Watch the topics length budget (~4000 hard cap); prefer surgical re-paragraphing over bulk addition. Convergent with the same-cycle ChatGPT review's "fairness/steelman" theme. See [[reviews/outer-review-2026-05-27-claude-opus-4-7]].

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Methodology proposals from outer reviews 2026-05-27 (opponent-reply, citation-integrity, novelty-audit, prior-art-credit, named-position-fidelity, taxonomy-consistency)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-05-27, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude]. Both reviewers independently proposed deep-review-skill additions that the meaning-of-life defects would have caught earlier; their lists overlap (opponent-reply / citation-integrity from ChatGPT; novelty-audit / prior-art-credit / named-position-fidelity from Claude — and named-position-fidelity is the methodology generalisation of the same "engage the actual position" finding both flagged at the article level). **ChatGPT 5.5 Pro**: "Add an opponent-reply requirement to deep reviews ... Add a citation-integrity check: every named philosopher in body text must appear in references." **Claude Opus 4.7**: "Add a 'claim-novelty audit' pass ... Add a 'prior-art credit' check ... a standing rule that every named philosopher gets at least two sentences accurately characterising the strongest version of their position before any criticism." Target `project/` methodology pages and/or the deep-review SKILL guidance; treat as a project-doc refine, not a content article. Consolidated checklist to fold in: (1) **Opponent-reply requirement** — for each major objection, require one paragraph stating how the best current opponent would answer the Map's reply (complements [[direct-refutation-discipline]] and the steelman pattern). (2) **Citation-integrity check** — every named philosopher in body text must appear in references; titles, venues, issue numbers, years, DOIs verified; unverified refs tagged (the two verified citation errors in meaning-of-life are the motivating exhibit; consider whether [[calibration-audit-triple]] or the literature-drift workflow should own a periodic citation-integrity sweep). (3) **Claim-novelty audit** — when an article asserts "a distinctive answer" / "fifth option" / "novel," verify the conceptual space isn't already occupied in the literature (a novelty audit against Metz's SEP entry + Rawlette would have caught the fifth-option over-claim). (4) **Prior-art-credit check** — when the Map's position is substantially the extension of a named philosopher's published view, require an explicit credit-and-distinguishing-move paragraph (the [[phenomenal-value-realism]] / evaluative-phenomenal-character pages do this; meaning-of-life did not, and no skill rule forces parity). (5) **Named-position-fidelity rule** — every named philosopher gets ≥2 sentences accurately stating the *strongest* version of their position before criticism (would have prevented the Benatar/Landau/Nagel misfilings). (6) **Taxonomy-consistency check across articles** — detect cross-article tension on *category* claims, not just citation hygiene (here: "fifth option" vs the sibling article's "holdable within physicalism / Rawlette isn't a dualist"). (7) **Partial-disconfirmation calibration** — falsifiability sections should include partial/empirical disconfirmers, not only decisive worldview-level defeat (NOTE: meaning-of-life's "What Would Challenge This View?" section *already* includes two partial disconfirmers, so this is a general refinement, not a per-article defect). Also weigh ChatGPT's "bedrock disagreements — refresh on schedule" point against the current deep-review practice of classifying framework-level objections as not-to-be-re-flagged (see [[bedrock-clash-vs-absorption]] / [[outer-review-empirical-vs-methodological-freshness]]). Keep scope to project-doc updates; do not invent new automation. See [[reviews/outer-review-2026-05-27-chatgpt-5-5-pro]] and [[reviews/outer-review-2026-05-27-claude-opus-4-7]].

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Strengthen opponent engagement in topics/meaning-of-life.md (engage actual positions: illusionism, error theory, evolutionary debunking, subjective naturalism, compatibilism, neutral nihilism)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-05-27, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude]. Both reviewers independently judged the article engages opponent *names* but not opponent *positions*, most damningly on illusionism. **ChatGPT 5.5 Pro**: "The illusionism response is rhetorically forceful but not sufficiently adversarial ... The article needs to engage that move directly rather than assuming that 'seeming' already entails phenomenal consciousness." **Claude Opus 4.7**: "the article writes as if illusionism has no response ... The article's rejoinder is therefore a question-begging restatement of phenomenal realism, not a refutation. A demanding referee would mark this section 'engages the position name but not the position.'" (Claude was deduplicated into this task; it adds the sharper error-theory and evolutionary-debunking horns.) Combined weaknesses, all verified present in the live article: (a) **Illusionism** — the regress response (line 149, "Misrepresentation presupposes presentation") assumes "seeming" already entails phenomenal consciousness; engage Frankish's "illusion problem" / diet-qualia move and Dennett directly (functional self-representation of phenomenality), and acknowledge it is contested by Kammerer's "rich illusion" account — do not present the regress as decisive. (b) **Error theory** — the current reply (Responding-to-Nihilism section) begs the question by restating the identity thesis; distinguish moral from prudential/eudaimonic value and address which queerness strands (supervenience, motivation — per Olson, *Moral Error Theory*, OUP 2014) the phenomenal-value pluralism inherits. (c) **Evolutionary debunking** — the article gives a *tracking* response but never confronts Street's *adaptive-link* horn: felt valence is itself a product of evolved hedonic architecture selected because it tracked fitness, so "felt-bad = intrinsic disvalue" risks being a just-so story. Add the adaptive-link horn and either answer it (why felt-badness is *constitutive* of disvalue, not merely correlated) or honestly mark it as an open problem on the falsifiability list. Cite Street, "A Darwinian Dilemma for Realist Theories of Value" (*Phil Studies* 127, 2006) and Joyce, *The Evolution of Morality* (MIT Press, 2006). (d) **Subjective naturalism** — the grass/hair-counting counterexamples (lines 53, 63) are easy targets; steelman sophisticated subjectivism (categorical desire, reflective endorsement, fitting-attitude, reasons-responsive accounts) before the Map's alternative. (e) **Compatibilist / hard-determinist meaning** — the article ties meaningful agency to nonphysical quantum influence (line 91) without engaging the live position that meaning survives determinism / loss of libertarian free will; add the objection and reply without implying compatibilist meaning is obviously impossible. (f) **Neutral nihilism** — the Responding-to-Nihilism section handles cosmic-despair and error-theory but not "neutral nihilism" (absence of cosmic telos as neither good nor bad, compatible with local mattering). **Apply the direct-refutation discipline** ([[direct-refutation-discipline]]): for each, decide whether the right move is defeating the opponent on its own terms, naming an unearned foundational move, or honest boundary-marking; **natural journal-quality prose** per [[writing-style]]'s "Engaging Opponents" section; **no mode labels in the body** (changelog only); if an in-framework refutation fails, say so in natural language. Watch the topics length budget (~3300 words, under the 4000 hard cap but tight) — prefer tightening existing prose; a condense follow-on may be warranted, and coordinate with the over-claiming/fifth-option tasks to avoid double-editing the same paragraphs. See [[reviews/outer-review-2026-05-27-chatgpt-5-5-pro]] and [[reviews/outer-review-2026-05-27-claude-opus-4-7]].

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Add evidential-status / tenet-dependency labelling to topics/meaning-of-life.md (calibrate quantum-agency and No-MWI over-claims)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-05-27, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude]. Both reviewers independently judged the article writes its strongest conclusions in a settled tone that outruns its tenets, the quantum/free-will grounding most of all — this is the article-level *quantum-over-claim* half of the convergent over-claiming critique (the *taxonomic*-over-claim half is the sibling "fifth option" P1 task — coordinate, the two together calibrate the article to its own honesty). **ChatGPT 5.5 Pro**: "the meaning article often writes as if rival views have been defeated rather than as if the conclusion is tenet-relative ... The claim that choices matter because consciousness influences quantum outcomes is the least-supported load-bearing claim." **Claude Opus 4.7** (Axis 4): "This is over-claimed in three distinct ways ... Speaking of the mechanism as if it is established physics is a slip from 'live interpretive option' to 'real causal structure' ... A compatibilist version of the article ... would be philosophically tighter and would not commit hostages to fortune in contested physics." Action: add a "Dependency and Evidential Status" treatment near "The Map's Position" marking each load-bearing claim as independently argued, tenet-dependent, speculative, or practical — in particular flag the quantum-agency and No-MWI meaning claims as tenet-dependent rather than established by the meaning argument alone, honouring [[evidential-status-discipline]]. Apply the calibrated phrasings the site already uses elsewhere (e.g. replace "consciousness influences which neural patterns become actual" with the contested-interpretive-proposal register; import the No-MWI calibration from the probability-problem-in-many-worlds article). Optionally note the luck-objection (Kane) and that the meaning thesis does not strictly *need* the quantum apparatus (Claude §5.3). **Apply the direct-refutation discipline** ([[direct-refutation-discipline]]): identify what kind of engagement each over-stated claim calls for — showing a rival defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move the framework has not earned, or honestly marking the framework-boundary disagreement (the No-MWI point is a Tenet-4 boundary marking, not a result the meaning argument delivers — a boundary-substitution risk). Reply in **natural journal-quality prose** per [[writing-style]]'s "Engaging Opponents" section; **no mode labels in the body** (changelog only). If an in-framework refutation fails, say so in natural language. See [[reviews/outer-review-2026-05-27-chatgpt-5-5-pro]] and [[reviews/outer-review-2026-05-27-claude-opus-4-7]].

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Fix verified citation errors and refresh stale literature in topics/meaning-of-life.md (Tallis issue, Landau 2025, missing references, post-2013 Metz)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-05-27, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt, claude]. Both reviewers flagged the References list as incomplete and behind the current literature (reference horizon Wolf 2010 / Metz 2013 / Nagel 1971). **ChatGPT 5.5 Pro** verified two citation errors during processing (see its Verification Notes): "The Tallis citation appears incorrect ... *Philosophy Now*, volume 161, April/May 2024" and "The Landau 2025 citation needs verification ... the 2025 *Journal of Philosophy of Life* special issue contents did not show that Landau title." **Claude Opus 4.7** flagged the missing post-2013 Metz work: "Add a literature-drift audit ... Metz, *God, Soul and the Meaning of Life* (Cambridge, 2019); Metz, 'The Concept of Life's Meaning,' ... (Oxford, 2022) ... All post-date the article's apparent reference horizon of Wolf 2010 / Metz 2013 / Nagel 1971." Combined actions: (1) **Tallis** — References list (line 286) cites "The Illusion of Illusionism. *Philosophy Now*, 159." The piece is actually in *Philosophy Now* **Issue 161 (April/May 2024)**, full title "Tallis in Wonderland: The Illusion of Illusionism," pp. 58–59. Fix 159→161 and consider adding the full column title and page range. (2) **Landau 2025** — citation "The Implications of Cosmic Meaninglessness, *Journal of Philosophy of Life*, 15(1), 2025" could not be verified; Landau's closest cosmic-context work is "Can Lives Be Seen as Meaningful Within the Cosmic Context?" (*Philosophia*, 2023, doi:10.1007/s11406-023-00660-x). Either replace with a verifiable Landau reference or remove the unverifiable one — do not leave a fabricated-looking citation. (3) **Missing in-body references** — Craig, Swinburne (line 47), Frankfurt, Taylor (line 61), Parfit (line 103), Sartre (line 151) are named in body text but absent from the References list; add proper references or note them inline. (4) **Add post-2013 Metz** — Metz, *God, Soul and the Meaning of Life* (Cambridge, 2019); Metz, "The Concept of Life's Meaning," in Landau (ed.), *The Oxford Handbook of Meaning in Life* (Oxford, 2022, pp. 27–42); and the revised 2021 SEP "Meaning of Life" entry. **VERIFY each bibliographic detail before adding** — Claude's page ranges/DOIs were not independently re-fetched this cycle. Citation hygiene + reference refresh only; do not touch argument structure (the Metz *engagement* deepening is a separate convergent task — coordinate, do not duplicate). See [[reviews/outer-review-2026-05-27-chatgpt-5-5-pro]] and [[reviews/outer-review-2026-05-27-claude-opus-4-7]].

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Fix overreaching IIT-recovery claim in topics/anaesthesia-and-the-consciousness-interface.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From research-topic 2026-05-27 → `research/pci-phi-anaesthesia-recovery-2026-05-27.md` (web-verified). The §"The Active Reboot" sentence — "Recent IIT work (2024–2025 PCI and Φ literature) finds integration measures recover *before* behavioural responsiveness, consistent with the workspace being prepared in advance" — **overreaches the literature** (constrain-vs-establish calibration error per [[evidential-status-discipline]]). The 2024–2025 PCI/Φ papers establish state-*dissociation* (integration stays high in unresponsive states) but are essentially silent on the fine-grained *timing* of integration-recovery vs. behaviour-return; the temporal-ordering claim is live-hypothesis at best, and attributing it to "recent IIT work" is inaccurate. The genuine "transient state precedes emergence" finding is **Lewis et al. (2018), *eLife* 7:e33250** (a sleep-like K-complex sensory-gating state — different mechanism, 2018 not 2024–2025). FIX (per the research note's recommendation): either (a) narrow the sentence to the *dissociation* claim and cite the verified state-dissociation papers — Onoda et al. 2025 (*Neurosci. Consciousness* 2025(1):niaf024), Breyton et al. 2025 (*eLife* 13:RP98920), Van Maldegem et al. 2025 (*Br. J. Anaesth.* 134(4):1088–1104, the best dissociation anchor) — OR (b) keep a precedence framing but anchor it explicitly on Lewis 2018 with corrected mechanism/date. Do NOT cite the 2024–2025 PCI/Φ papers *as* support for the timing claim. All citations in the research note are web-verified with DOIs/PMIDs — use them verbatim. Citation + calibration hygiene; preserve the article's argument. Verify scripts/sync.py exits 0.

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Write project-tier methodology page on Convergence-Arguments Discipline
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: From optimistic-2026-05-19b §"Ideas for Later" (line 163). The `concepts/cross-cultural-convergence-on-mental-causation.md` article uses convergence-as-evidence cleanly and its §"Why Convergence Matters Epistemically" sketches three conditions a strong convergence argument must satisfy (independence condition, methodological-diversity condition, against-the-grain condition). The discipline is also at issue in pessimistic-2026-05-19b Issue 3 (convergence-double-counting in consciousness-and-skill-acquisition — four lines making the same evidential move not being four-fold evidence) — independently flagged by the altered-state symmetry discipline's checklist for filter-theory articles. The discipline recurs across the catalogue: `apex/the-convergence-argument-for-dualism.md` is its load-bearing apex; `concepts/cross-mechanism-convergence.md` (created today) is its sister discipline at the mechanism-convergence grain; `concepts/cross-cultural-convergence-on-mental-causation.md` is its cross-cultural exhibit. Write a project-tier methodology page that (a) names the discipline cleanly — *convergence-arguments discipline*: the constraints a convergence argument must satisfy to count as multiple-evidence rather than one-pattern-expressed-N-times; (b) catalogues the three conditions — independence (the converging lines must not share their evidential move); methodological-diversity (the lines must arrive at the convergent reading via genuinely distinct methods); against-the-grain (the convergence must hold despite incentives to diverge); (c) names the catalogue's two distinct convergence-failure modes — convergence-double-counting (cited in pessimistic-2026-05-19b Issue 3) and convergence-by-accident (sketched in `concepts/cross-mechanism-convergence.md` §"Failure Mode: Convergence-by-Accident"); (d) walks through 3–4 worked exhibits — the cross-cultural-convergence on mental causation case (passes independence, methodological-diversity, against-the-grain); the consciousness-and-skill-acquisition four-converging-lines case (fails independence — recently flagged by pessimistic review); the cross-tradition meditation convergence case (passes most conditions but is now in tension with the cessation-vs-plenitude divergence); the cross-mechanism convergence case at the mechanism grain; (e) names the relationship to existing discipline pages — [[evidential-status-discipline]], [[concepts/cross-mechanism-convergence]] (sister discipline at the mechanism-convergence grain), [[project/common-cause-null]] (the convergence-evaluation companion at the catalogue grain), [[project/coherence-inflation-countermeasures]] (the systemic-coherence companion); (f) closes by stating what the discipline does *not* claim — it does not (yet) deliver a quantitative discount factor for convergence-failures; it disciplines the way convergence-arguments are cited so that strong claims rest on convergences that pass all three conditions and tentative claims acknowledge convergences that fail one or more. Cross-link bidirectionally with all named partners. Estimated scope: 1,500–2,000 words. Tenet alignment: methodological — applies across all five tenets equally; the discipline is about evidence-pattern strength, not tenet-specific commitment. Target section: `project/` (no cap) — natural home alongside `[[common-cause-null]]`, `[[voids-circularity-discount]]`, `[[evidential-status-discipline]]`, `[[direct-refutation-discipline]]`. See `reviews/optimistic-2026-05-19b.md` §"Ideas for Later" line 163, `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19b.md` Issue 3, and `concepts/cross-mechanism-convergence.md`.

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Write topic article on Q3-Q4 Sliding Boundary and the Transparency Problem
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19. From optimistic-2026-05-19d §"Medium Priority — Q3-Q4 sliding boundary and the transparency problem" (lines 135–140). The mechanism-costs-dualism-thickness-quadrants article (§"Q3", lines 89–99) notes that "a genuine Q3 dualism — mind thick, physics thin, but both ontologically real — appears rare in the literature precisely because, once the mind does this much work, the physical side tends to lose explanatory weight and the position seems to slide toward Q3-monism." This is a sharp methodological observation that deserves its own treatment: which positions sit *stably* at Q3 dualism vs slide toward Q3 monism, and what holds them in place. Kastrup's analytic idealism slides to monism; Berkeley's idealism is straight monism; Myers-James subliminal-mind with lean physics may be the rare Q3-stable dualism. A dedicated treatment would let the four-quadrant taxonomy's Q3 cell stop being mostly-uninhabited. Write a topic-tier article that (a) names the sliding-boundary problem cleanly — *Q3 transparency problem*: when the mind does most of the explanatory work and the physical side is thin, the physical side tends to lose ontological weight and the position slides from dualism to idealism; what (if anything) stabilises Q3 dualism against this slide; (b) walks through the historical record of Q3 positions and their trajectories — Berkeley (started monist); Leibniz's monadology (idealist-leaning); Kastrup's analytic idealism (explicitly monist); Whitehead's process philosophy (Q3-leaning but slides); Myers's subliminal-mind and James's neutral monism (the rare candidates for Q3-stable dualism); Tibetan Buddhist mind-only schools (slide to non-dual); (c) names the structural reasons for the slide — when explanation accumulates on the mental side, parsimony pressure on retaining a separate physical substance grows; physical thinness reduces the explanatory burden physics could bear, undermining the rationale for keeping it ontologically distinct; the four-quadrant taxonomy makes this visible at the Q3 cell; (d) considers what would stabilise Q3 dualism — selection-only channel commitments (physics does *some* work, just less); bidirectional-interaction symmetry requirements (the channel must run both ways, which requires both sides to be real); the Map's tenets considered together (Tenet 2's minimal-quantum-interaction commitment and Tenet 3's bidirectional-interaction commitment may jointly stabilise Q3); (e) names the analogies to MWI's branch-explanation problem (the transparency problem has structural similarities to MWI's question of which branch counts as the actual one); (f) closes by identifying the Map's own positioning — likely Q3-stable or Q3/Q4-border, with the tenets jointly stabilising against the slide. Honor [[evidential-status-discipline]] throughout. Cross-links to [[mechanism-costs-dualism-thickness-quadrants]], [[four-quadrant-dualism-taxonomy]], [[analytic-idealism]] (if exists), [[neutral-monism]] (if exists), [[interactionist-dualism]], [[selection-only-channel]] (if exists), [[bidirectional-interaction]], [[no-many-worlds]]. Estimated scope: 2,000–3,000 words. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — the article clarifies what stable Q3 *dualism* requires); Tenet 4 (No Many Worlds — the transparency problem's structure has analogies to MWI's branch-explanation problem); Tenet 2 + Tenet 3 (jointly stabilising the Map's Q3 commitment). Target section: `topics/` (currently 247/250 — 3 slots remain; this article exhausts a critical methodological gap in the four-quadrant cluster). See `reviews/optimistic-2026-05-19d.md` §"Medium Priority" (lines 135–140).

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Write concept article on the Lived/Objectified Body Distinction (Leib/Körper)
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19. From optimistic-2026-05-19d §"Medium Priority — Christina's case and the lived/objectified body distinction" (lines 128–133). The Husserl/Merleau-Ponty distinction between *Leib* (lived body / *corps propre*) and *Körper* (objectified body / *corps objectif*) is currently introduced only briefly in `concepts/somatic-interface.md` §"Proprioception" (lines 63–67). A dedicated concept-tier treatment would let the catalogue route other embodiment-articles — `topics/phantom-limb-phenomena.md`, `topics/embodied-consciousness.md`, `voids/embodiment-cognitive-limits.md`, `concepts/embodied-cognition.md`, `topics/dual-domain-capabilities-in-proprioception-and-spatial-imagination.md`, `topics/leibnizs-mill-argument.md` — through a single canonical statement of the distinction rather than re-deriving it inline. Christina's case (Oliver Sacks's patient who lost proprioception and reported that she felt "disembodied") is the clinical paradigm; the article could integrate Sacks's other relevant case-studies (*A Leg to Stand On*, the disembodied lady case, the man who fell out of bed) as additional clinical anchors. Write a concept-tier page that (a) names the distinction cleanly — *lived body vs objectified body*: the felt, first-person body inhabited (*Leib* / *corps propre*) is phenomenologically distinct from the objective body observed from outside (*Körper* / *corps objectif*); (b) anchors the distinction in Husserl's *Ideen II* and Merleau-Ponty's *Phénoménologie de la perception* as the canonical phenomenological statements; (c) walks through 3–4 worked clinical exhibits — Christina's case (proprioception loss); phantom-limb phenomena (Leib without Körper); locked-in syndrome (Körper without expressive Leib); training and somatic awareness practices (Leib's plasticity); (d) names the distinction's relationship to the catalogue's dualism reading — the lived body is *not reducible* to the objectified body but is *bidirectionally coupled* with it (Tenet 1 + Tenet 3); (e) cross-links bidirectionally to [[somatic-interface]], [[embodied-cognition]], [[phantom-limb-phenomena]], [[embodied-consciousness]], [[embodiment-cognitive-limits]], [[proprioception]] (if exists), [[interoceptive-void]]; (f) closes by stating what the distinction does *not* claim — it does not require substance dualism; multiple metaphysical readings accommodate the phenomenological distinction. Honor [[evidential-status-discipline]] throughout. Estimated scope: 1,500–2,500 words. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — the lived body resists reduction to the objectified body); Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction — consciousness modulates the felt sense of inhabiting a body, evidenced by training, dissociative states, phantom limbs). Target section: `concepts/` (currently 246/250 — 4 slots remain). See `reviews/optimistic-2026-05-19d.md` §"Medium Priority" (lines 128–133).

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Harden ledger completeness and standalone readability in topics/forward-in-time-vs-time-symmetric-selection.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-27 found the new comparison article publication-sound (citations web-verified, no label leakage, calibration well-held) but with four light-touch quality gaps, each fixable in 1-3 sentences without disturbing voice or the non-adjudication discipline. (1) The non-adjudication mandate is stated so emphatically it risks reading as a comparison engineered to produce no comparison — add a sentence naming what a reader is *entitled* to do with the ledger (their own priors do the weighing; the Map declines only to impose a tie-breaker). (2) "Each route forecloses exactly what the other keeps" overstates the partition — soften and acknowledge intermediate readings (formal-only time-symmetry, the modified growing block) blur the foreclosure. (3) "Counterfactual exclusion" is asserted as the shared MWI-rejecting floor without defence — add a clause sourcing it to the indexical-identity argument in [[many-worlds-argument]] so the standalone document doesn't beg the question against the MWI defender. (4) The amplification step (quantum-scale bias → neural behaviour) is deferred parenthetically rather than entered as a line item — promote it to an explicit cost in the mechanism-costs section. Also: caveat the unreplicated status of Hagan et al. 2002 where the favourable estimate is cited, and consider citing/softening the two uncited foundations generalisations (TI as minority position; physicists declining to read time-symmetry as causally real). See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-27-forward-vs-time-symmetric]].

- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19. From optimistic-2026-05-19c §"Medium Priority — Forward-in-time vs time-symmetric selection comparison article" (lines 130–135). The `concepts/time-symmetric-physics.md` article §"The Libet Problem" (lines 122–128) now names the forward-in-time conscious selection family as a genuine alternative carrying different mechanistic costs, but the *comparison* of the two routes is not developed in any single article — it is distributed across the time-symmetric piece, `topics/forward-in-time-conscious-selection.md`, `apex/post-decoherence-selection-programme.md`, and `concepts/mechanism-costs-cartography.md`. A focused topic article would gather the comparison at full resolution. Write a topic-tier article that (a) names the comparison cleanly — *forward-in-time vs time-symmetric conscious selection*: two route-families for how consciousness participates in quantum-level selection, differing on whether selection operates within standard forward causation or via time-symmetric (transactional / retrocausal) interpretations; (b) walks through what each route requires metaphysically — forward-in-time selection requires no retrocausal commitment but inherits the standard collapse-mechanism problem; time-symmetric selection requires the transactional interpretation (Cramer 1986, Kastner 2012) and absorbs the Libet timing problem by allowing atemporal selection; (c) walks through what each route requires physically — forward-in-time requires a mechanism for selection at the moment of decoherence (the Sustained Attention / Zeno mechanism with its decoherence-gap problem); time-symmetric requires a mechanism for atemporal participation in transactions (Stapp's Zeno mechanism repurposed for transactional selection); (d) walks through what empirical handles each makes available — forward-in-time predicts that selection events should be detectable at decoherence boundaries; time-symmetric predicts that delayed-choice and Wheeler-type experiments should show the expected pattern (Vedovato 2017 is the current state-of-art); (e) walks through what philosophical commitments each forecloses — forward-in-time forecloses time-symmetric metaphysics but preserves standard temporal becoming; time-symmetric forecloses presentism but preserves the Libet-friendly atemporal-selection reading; (f) walks through where each route stands on the Map's mechanism-costs accounting (per `concepts/mechanism-costs-cartography.md`) — both pay the decoherence-gap cost; time-symmetric pays an additional cost in retrocausal metaphysics; forward-in-time pays an additional cost in the standard-collapse-mechanism debt; (g) closes by stating the Map's posture — both routes are held as live alternatives rather than rivals (per `concepts/time-symmetric-physics.md` line 128); the comparison clarifies what each costs without adjudicating between them; (h) honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] throughout — the comparison is at the *mechanism-cost-accounting* register, not at the *empirical-establishment* register. Cross-link bidirectionally to [[time-symmetric-physics]], [[forward-in-time-conscious-selection]], [[post-decoherence-selection-programme]], [[mechanism-costs-cartography]], [[sustained-attention]], [[quantum-zeno-effect]], [[the-libet-problem]] (if exists), [[transactional-interpretation]] (if exists). Estimated scope: 2,500–4,000 words. Tenet alignment: Tenet 2 (MQI — both routes are MQI-shaped mechanisms); Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction — both routes describe how consciousness influences physical outcomes); Tenet 4 (No Many Worlds — both routes are MWI-incompatible). Target section: `topics/` (currently 247/250 — 3 slots remain; verify cap state before placement; if this task and the affective-tone task above both target topics/ when only one slot remains, prefer this one as it closes a more substantial cluster gap). Estimated scope: 1,500–2,500 words. - **Type**: expand-topic

Estimated scope: 2,500–3,500 words. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — psychogenic disconnection without structural damage is the cleanest interface-reading-favouring exhibit); Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction — conversion disorder demonstrates that consciousness-to-brain causation has discrete components that can fail independently). - **Notes**: Suggested by optimistic review 2026-05-25. The per-cluster independence-scoring programme currently rests on N=2 worked exhibits — the surplus-void (retracted) and the introspection-architecture sub-cluster (partially promoted). As [[topics/introspection-architecture-independence-scoring]] admits in its own words, "the discrimination is consistent with both *methodology works* and *methodology rationalises decisions already made*; stronger non-flatness claims await an unfavourable test case." Choose a void cluster the method would be expected to score *low* on (weak framework-independence, convergence running through framework definitions) and score it honestly. If it scores low, the method discriminates; the credibility of the whole voids-circularity-discount programme depends on producing such a case. Highest-leverage move available to the evidential-status programme. UPGRADED SCOPE (pessimistic review 2026-05-25b, Issue 2): the N=2 self-validation worry the introspection-architecture exhibit names ("consistent with both methodology works and methodology rationalises decisions already made") is *not discharged by a third exhibit scored by the same hand applying the same unstated standard*. To count as a genuine non-flatness test, the third exhibit must be scored against an explicit rubric (see the paired P2 rubric task) and/or blind to the desired outcome — author-graded confirmation reproduces the very circularity it is meant to break. Until then, comparative-score claims ("higher than the surplus-void") should not be asserted as findings. See reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-25b.md.

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Develop the Penrose objective-reduction intersection as the reverse inference's empirical horizon
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Suggested by optimistic review 2026-05-25c (High Priority). [[topics/the-reverse-inference]] §"No Empirical Consequences" answers its single most-cited objection (no empirical consequences) with a one-clause gesture: "regimes where gravitational self-energy intersects with collapse (Penrose-style objective reduction), where consciousness-modulated collapse might diverge from purely physical collapse in detectable ways." This names but does not develop the framework's empirical horizon. A dedicated treatment would (a) survey what an Orch-OR-style or consciousness-modulated divergence from purely physical objective reduction would have to look like; (b) report current experimental bounds (e.g. Donadi et al. 2021 constraints on gravitational/CSL collapse, diorganic-Penrose-style tests) and what they already rule out; (c) honour [[direct-refutation-discipline]] and [[evidential-status-discipline]] — frame as "untested, not untestable," naming what a *discriminating* result would be rather than claiming one. IMPORTANT pre-execution check: this borders [[concepts/born-rule-and-the-consciousness-interface]] and any existing objective-collapse article — verify coverage first; if substantial overlap exists, convert to a refine-draft adding a section to the reverse-inference or born-rule article rather than a standalone. Section: topics/ — verify cap before placement. Estimated scope: 1500–2500 words. Tenet alignment: Tenet 2 (Minimal Quantum Interaction), Tenet 4 (No Many Worlds). See [[reviews/optimistic-2026-05-25c]].

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Give "intrinsic nature" a concept-level treatment (or fold into the existing void article)
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Suggested by optimistic review 2026-05-26 (High Priority). The structural-ontological gap in [[concepts/physical-completeness]] turns entirely on *intrinsic nature* — what relational structure leaves undescribed — and the article treats it as a load-bearing primitive, yet the only target is [[voids/intrinsic-nature-void]], which frames it as unfillable-in-principle. There is no neutral concept page defining intrinsic nature and surveying the three readings the cohort keeps invoking: structural realism (Ladyman/French/Worrall — there is no residual intrinsic nature, or none we can know), Russellian monism ([[concepts/russellian-monism]] — consciousness *is* the intrinsic nature of the physical), and the Map's dualism (intrinsic nature exists and is ontologically distinct from structure, where consciousness enters). The wikilink resolves by basename so this is NOT a broken-link issue — it is a missing concept-level home that `physical-completeness`, `russellian-monism`, and `bi-aspectual-ontology` all silently assume. **Section-cap caveat**: concepts/ stands near 246/250; verify the cap at execution. If no slot is free, fold the definitional treatment into [[voids/intrinsic-nature-void]] as a "what intrinsic nature is" preamble before its unfillability argument, and convert this to a refine-draft rather than creating a new page. Keep the Maudlin objection (intrinsic-natures-already-in-physics) visible — it is the framework-boundary opponent `physical-completeness` already names. Estimated scope: Medium. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (dualism), Tenet 5 (Occam's limits — the structuralist reading is a bet, not a result). See [[reviews/optimistic-2026-05-26]].

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Address calibration gaps in topics/indian-philosophy-of-mind.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: PROMOTED P3→P2 by /replenish-queue 2026-05-27 (one of three floor-restoring promotions — actionable P0-P2 queue stood at 2, below the MIN_QUEUE floor of 3, against a ~464-task P3 backlog; per the documented steady-state pattern, replenish promotes concrete review-flagged refines / staleness deep-reviews rather than minting speculative expand/apex/project-doc work, and promotes several at once to break the per-iteration alternation). This is the most-actionable refine in the backlog: four small, already-scoped in-place fixes from a named pessimistic review, no structural rework, no length risk (article well under the topics hard cap, last_deep_review 2026-04-24, ai_contribution=100). Pessimistic review 2026-05-26 (see [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-26-indian-philosophy-of-mind]]) found four small, in-place fixes — no structural rework needed; the article's §"The Three-Way Disagreement" empiricist concession is exemplary and must be PRESERVED verbatim. Fixes: (1) LOW — §Ramanuja's Critique states "Ramanuja (1017–1137 CE)" as bare fact; these are the *traditional* Sri Vaishnava dates (120-year lifespan), modern scholarship (Carman) favours 1077–1157; add a "traditionally" hedge. (2) MEDIUM — the §"Relation to Site Perspective" Dualism and Occam paragraphs re-lean on the convergence as strong evidence ("one would expect at least one major school… to have found reduction viable") after §Three-Way explicitly downgraded it and granted the common-cause reading "force"; add a back-reference clause so the strong inference is not made twice unqualified. (3) MEDIUM — §"Arguments for the Self" presents the Nyaya memory/cross-modal/unity arguments as defeating the Buddhist bundle theory ("cannot explain this convergence") and lets them stand unrebutted, in tension with the site's own serious treatment of *anatman* in [[buddhism-and-dualism]] and [[void-as-ground-of-meaning]]; add one sentence noting the Madhyamaka causal-continuity rejoinder (continuity ≠ identity) and that the debate remains live. (4) LOW — §No Many Worlds calls the plurality-of-selves question "precisely the vertiginous question"; plurality of co-real simultaneous selves is orthogonal to branch-relative indexing — soften "precisely" to "anticipates the structure of" and note the disanalogy. Verified clean: no label leakage, altered-state symmetry audit N/A, sapta-vidha anupapatti correctly attributed. Tenet alignment: 1, 3, 4, 5.

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Fix choking-section structure and soften empirical claims in embodied-cognition.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: RE-HOMED to Active Tasks + PROMOTED P3→P2 by /replenish-queue 2026-05-27 (one of four floor-restoring promotions). The actionable (P0–P2) queue stood at 1 (the consciousness-and-skill-acquisition literature-drift P2, which runs on its own weekly wall-clock schedule and the parser counts as OTHER), below the MIN_QUEUE floor of 3; per steady-state guidance the honest move is to promote ~4 grounded review-flagged P3 tasks rather than mint speculative expand-topic/condense work against at/over-cap sections (topics 247/270, concepts 245/270, voids 101/100) and a ~500-task P3 backlog. This task's prior copy was marooned below the `## Vetoed Tasks` header (the loop parser in tools/todo/processor.py classifies anything after that header as vetoed and never executes it), so it was copied here to make it live. The most surgical of the four: a single-file, fully-specified pessimistic-review fix, lowest-risk and cap-safe. Pessimistic review (2026-05-26) found four issues in [[concepts/embodied-cognition]], all non-critical. (1) MEDIUM / truncation-resilience — line 97 stages the inference "If consciousness were epiphenomenal... step 2 couldn't follow from step 1" as if it goes through, but line 103 concedes the choking data "does not provide evidence *for* [bidirectional interaction] over the standard physicalist account." A truncating LLM reader gets the over-claim without the retraction. Reframe line 97 to lead with the qualification ("It is tempting to read this as evidence against epiphenomenalism, but the inference does not survive scrutiny:") so structure matches conclusion. (2) LOW — soften the choking empirical bullets (lines 90-94): "Research confirms:" → "Research has found:"; attribute "skills learned without explicit awareness are protected" to Masters (1992) reinvestment line; hedge the contested "highly skilled choke more" and "dual-task can improve performance" claims with "some studies find." (3) LOW — the "No Many Worlds" tenet section (line 191) is a placeholder that outsources entirely to [[mental-effort]]; either state honestly that embodiment is orthogonal to the MWI question or trim. (4) LOW — soften "sit comfortably within a dualist framework" (L103, L137) to "is consistent with"; the fit relies on the antenna analogy the article itself flags as question-begging. PRESERVE the choking section's self-correction (line 103), the falsificationist "What Would Challenge This View?" section, and the Nāgārjuna/antenna open-question honesty. Article is clean of label-leakage and does NOT trigger the altered-state symmetry audit. See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-26-embodied-cognition]].

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Reconcile the Christian List "Many-Worlds Theory of Consciousness" citation across the corpus (corpus-split propagation)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: PROMOTED P3→P2 by /replenish-queue 2026-05-27 (one of four floor-restoring promotions — see brain-specialness-boundary above for the queue rationale). Re-verified 2026-05-27: the corpus-split persists across 7 files (grep "Many-Worlds Theory of Consciousness"). Citation reconciliation is the corpus's dominant substantive-defect channel and a corpus-split is the high-signal tell that at least one side is wrong, so this is a grounded, high-value refine rather than speculative work. Surfaced by the 2026-05-26 deep review of `concepts/indexical-knowledge-and-identity.md` (commit a723bb42), which web-verified the authoritative form and fixed it in that article. The same paper is cited with conflicting metadata across ≥5 other files — years 2022 / 2023 / 2025, volume 57(2) vs 57(4), and page ranges 316-340 / 329-356 / 789-812. **Authoritative form (web-verified, DOI 10.1111/nous.12408): List, C. (2023). "The Many-Worlds Theory of Consciousness." *Noûs* 57(2), 316-340. (First published online 2022.)** Known offenders to reconcile: `research/voids-tenet-generated-voids-2026-01-25.md` (2022, no vol/pp), `research/voids-perspectival-void-2026-02-20.md` (2023, 57(4)), `research/indexical-identity-haecceity-thisness-2026-01-23.md` (2025), `research/voids-modal-void-2026-02-16.md` (2023, 57(2) 329-356), `research/consciousness-non-collapsed-quantum-states-2026-02-10.md` (2022, 57(4) 789-812). **CRITICAL — do NOT conflate** with the *separate* List paper "A Quadrilemma for Theories of Consciousness" (*The Philosophical Quarterly* 2024, cited in `topics/vertiginous-question.md:208`); that is a different work and stays as-is. Most offenders are `research/` working notes, but they feed `expand-topic`, so wrong metadata can propagate into new published articles — grep the whole corpus and fix every instance. Tenet alignment: citation accuracy / [[evidential-status-discipline]]. See memory: AI citation metadata unreliable.

### ✓ 2026-05-27: Address structural gaps in topics/personal-identity.md (pessimistic review 2026-05-27)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-27 (oldest-reviewed Tenet-4 load-bearing topic; last_deep_review 2026-04-18, never previously pessimistic-reviewed). Three actionable findings, all framing/argument-completeness rather than factual error: (1) **MEDIUM — fission/branching asymmetry** (lines 76 vs 132-134): the article dissolves the fission question with "identity can't branch" logic but treats the structurally identical quantum-branching question ("which branch am I in?") as meaningful and answer-having. Add one paragraph distinguishing the cases on principled grounds OR concede the symmetry. This is the sharpest internal tension and gives a Deutsch-style critic free leverage. (2) **MEDIUM — "seeming requires a subject" regress deployed 3x unrebutted** (lines 98, 164, 166): the article's primary anti-haecceity/anti-illusionism weapon never states-and-answers the standard Dennettian/Frankish reply (the apparent subject is itself represented content). State the deflationary reply once, respond, then have the three deployments cross-reference it rather than re-assert. Link [[illusionism]] / [[apophatic-cartography]] which carry related regress work. (3) **LOW — possible boundary-substitution** (line 68, "The tenets commit us to a stronger view"): per [[direct-refutation-discipline]], reword so the tenet-commitment is named as a foundational starting point, with the actual in-framework argument-against-Parfit located explicitly in the haecceity section (it exists downstream — this is a framing fix). Also: soften "establishes"/"shows" overstatements at lines 90, 210, 190 ([[evidential-status-discipline]]); add a citation/cross-link for the bare "decoherence calculations may be off by orders of magnitude" claim (line 174) to [[quantum-neural-timing-constraints]]; clarify the causal-history argument (lines 92/116/214) gives *content* to an already-posited indexical particular rather than *generating* haecceity (otherwise it collapses into ordinary numerical non-identity Parfit already accepts). NOTE: the long-stale todo item (this file, the "Propagate unfalsifiability caveat" open block) is already SATISFIED for personal-identity.md (caveat present lines 104, 212) and should be closed; the Santander 2025 citation (line 257) is correct post corpus-split fix. Tenet alignment: Tenet 4 (No Many Worlds / indexical identity) primary, Tenet 1 (Dualism). See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-27-personal-identity]].

### ✓ 2026-05-26: Recalibrate the four load-bearing empirical citations in phenomenal-output-causal-machinery-dissociation.md (Naccache, Hagger, Cleary, Tulving)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From outer review 2026-05-26 (Claude Opus 4.7), recommendations A1–A4 + B4. Verified against the live article that each citation is under-hedged. (A3) Naccache et al. 2005 is presented as plain evidence; flag it as a **single-patient case study** with a large unilateral mesio-frontal lesion (not focal ACC) whose patient also showed Iowa-Gambling-Task deficits and absent SCRs, with no direct replication (cite Fellows & Farah 2005, Cipolotti et al. 2016 for the mixed ACC-lesion literature). (A4) The Hagger 2016 line says it "collapsed the strength-resource model" with no hedge; recalibrate to cite both Hagger (d=0.04, [−0.07,0.15]) *and* Vohs et al. 2021 (d=0.06, BF₀₊≈4.39) and Dang et al. 2021 (12 labs, N≈1,775, d=0.10, [0.01,0.19], different task pairing showing a small significant effect), and note Hagger's own caveat against fully rejecting depletion. (A2) Recover the fuller Cleary déjà-vu programme — Cleary & Claxton 2018 found déjà vu produces an illusory *feeling* of knowing the next turn while predictions stayed at chance, plus Cleary et al. 2019's postdictive bias; this is mildly unfriendly to the clean "verdict is the explanandum" reading and should be acknowledged, not omitted. (A1) Add a paragraph on Tulving's autonoetic consciousness / the remember-know distinction as a *phenomenology of retrieval mode* that complicates the clean "outputs not operations" picture — either bite the bullet (autonoetic feeling is itself a mode-tagging verdict-output) or concede the recollection case is less clean; note Sant'Anna 2024's "episodic memory without autonoetic consciousness" challenge. **Web-verify every effect size, CI, and the Cleary/Sant'Anna findings before inserting** (see Verification Notes in the review). Tenet alignment: citation accuracy / calibration honesty.

### ✓ 2026-05-26: Propagate Garrison 2013 + Rodriguez-Larios 2020 citation fixes across the meditation cluster
- **Type**: refine-draft

(1) **Hasenkamp → Garrison**: the 2013 "Effortless awareness: using real time neurofeedback..." paper (*Frontiers in Human Neuroscience*, 7, 440) is first-authored by **Kathleen A. Garrison** (Garrison, Santoyo, Davis, Thornhill, Kerr, Brewer); Wendy Hasenkamp was the editor, not an author — a prominent-name-magnet conflation with the real Hasenkamp et al. (2012) *NeuroImage* 59(1):750-760 mind-wandering paper (correctly cited in `phenomenology-of-returning-attention.md`). (2) **Wong → Rodriguez-Larios + wrong article number**: the 2020 *Scientific Reports* paper "From thoughtless awareness to effortful cognition: alpha-theta cross-frequency dynamics..." is authored by **Julio Rodriguez-Larios et al.** (no author named Wong), article number **5419** not 2095 (DOI 10.1038/s41598-020-62392-2). Files still carrying errors: `concepts/meditation-and-consciousness-modes.md` (BOTH errors, ref lines ~285-286); `concepts/witness-consciousness.md` (Wong→Rodriguez-Larios ONLY at ref line ~227 — Garrison already correct there at line 218); `research/meditation-observer-witness-phenomenon-2026-01-18.md` (BOTH, ref lines ~286, 297 — fix to stop re-propagation). After fixing, grep the corpus for residual `Hasenkamp, W., et al. (2013)`, `Wong, K. F.`, and `10, 2095` to confirm full cleanup. Citation integrity only, not build-breaking.

### ✓ 2026-05-26: Propagate corrected Santander et al. (2025) PNAS citation across the split-brain cluster
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Discovered during the 2026-05-26 deep review of `topics/split-brain-consciousness.md`. The 2025 PNAS minimal-callosal-fibers study is mis-cited across the corpus as "Miller, M. et al. (2025). Even minimal fiber connections can unify consciousness. *PNAS*." Web-verified primary source (PubMed 41118210; doi 10.1073/pnas.2520190122): first author is **Tyler Santander** (NOT Miller — Miller is the *senior/last* author; M.S. Gazzaniga is a co-author), the real title is **"Full interhemispheric integration sustained by a fraction of posterior callosal fibers"** *PNAS* **122(43), e2520190122**, published **October 28, 2025** (epub Oct 21) — NOT "November 2025". The press-release headline ("Even minimal fiber connections can unify consciousness") was used as the paper title, and several files use the UCSB news URL in place of the DOI. `topics/split-brain-consciousness.md` is now FIXED (reference + body date + an internal "2025 layered model" mislabel). Propagate the correct citation to: `topics/personal-identity.md` (lines ~140, ~257), `topics/neurological-dissociations-as-interface-architecture.md` (lines ~116, ~225), `topics/experimental-consciousness-science-2025-2026.md` (line ~127), `apex/identity-across-transformations.md` (line ~200), `concepts/substrate-independence.md` (line ~234), and the source research note `research/split-brain-consciousness-unity-2026-01-14.md` (line ~42; also has author-affiliation errors — Gazzaniga/Miller are UCSB, Sporns is Indiana, Volz is Cologne). Citation metadata only — the body substance (250M axons; ~1cm/minimal posterior fibers sustain full integration; rerouting across remaining ribbon) is accurate everywhere. Corpus-split tell: defect originated in the research note and propagated outward. See [[reviews/deep-review-2026-05-26-split-brain-consciousness]].

### ✓ 2026-05-26: Address pessimistic-review findings in concepts/functionalism.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-26 (first dedicated pessimistic review of the site's oldest concept). Four findings: (1) **Citation defect, Medium** — line 73 / ref list line 201: inline text claims COGITATE "tested GWT against IIT; neither theory's predictions were fully confirmed" and dates it "2023-2024", but the only reference is Melloni et al. 2023 PLOS ONE e0268577, which is the *protocol* paper (no results). Add the results citation, verified today as **Cogitate Consortium / Melloni et al. 2025, *Nature* 642:133-142**, keeping the 2023 protocol for the protocol mention. (2) **Internal tension, Low** — line 89 "The failure of functionalism reframes the AI question" asserts failure as settled, contradicting line 87 which leaves the Chinese Room dialectically open ("may beg the question — exactly what's at issue"); soften to conditional. (3) **Conceivability premise, Medium** — line 173's "the conceivability arguments would remain" makes conceivability the final court of appeal, yet line 99 asserts "if either is possible, functionalism is false" without acknowledging the contested conceivability→possibility inference (type-B physicalist response); add one clause with a pointer. (4) **Concession-convergence confirmation-bias, Medium** — lines 69/81 read both Putnam's pragmatism and biological computationalism as retreats toward dualism, but substrate-dependence is fully compatible with physicalism; state the convergence as a Map-favoured interpretation, not a forced reading. Also: a Medium reasoning-mode item — the article under-deploys the strongest in-framework pressure (functionalism helps itself to "functional organization is sufficient for consciousness" without earning the sufficiency claim by its own standards); make that crisp. Preserve untouched: the Specification Problem section (lines 115-121) and the phenomenal-non-compositionality paragraph (line 127) — the article's best material. See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-26-functionalism]].

### ✓ 2026-05-26: Address citation conflation and mechanism-gap double-standard in topics/presentiment-and-retrocausality.md
- **Type**: refine-draft

(2011) reanalyzing Bem (2011); the research note dates the BF to a 2025 Bem et al. mega meta-analysis not present in the reference list — pin to a cited source and resolve the 2011/2016/2025 conflation. (2) Med: mechanism-gap asymmetry (l.77) grants the Map's own neural interface "plausibly interfacing" while denying presentiment on scale grounds, but omits the Tegmark decoherence objection that threatens both — name it and make the asymmetry one of degree-of-implausibility, cross-link quantum-consciousness. (3) Med: l.103 "demonstrate retrocausal structure empirically" overstates delayed-choice (phenomenon confirmed, interpretation is one empirically-equivalent reading) — same double standard the article applies to presentiment. See pessimistic-2026-05-26c.md.

### ✓ 2026-05-26: Fold cross-observer non-Western-physicalist finding into concession-convergence §"Three Discriminating Tests"
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Unconsumed-research chain from today's `research-topic` output `research/non-western-physicalist-narrowing-cross-observer-2026-05-26.md`. The research tested the empirical claim at [[concepts/concession-convergence]] §"Three Discriminating Tests" (line 106) — that no non-Western tradition has produced a *physicalist theory of consciousness narrowing toward type-specificity-respecting specifications independently of the Western analytic literature* — and confirms it is a **gap** (no qualifying theory), now better grounded than the article's current one-sentence treatment. Four traditions surveyed, each failing a different conjunct of (physicalist) AND (narrows toward type-specificity under own internal logic) AND (independent of Western literature): contemporary Chinese philosophy of mind (qi-naturalism non-reductive; analytic work in Western dialogue → not independent); Japanese naturalized phenomenology (Taguchi/CHAIN, Yuasa — non-reductive/enactivist, openly engages Western theory); Buddhist Abhidharma citta-caitta (non-physicalist — dharma-categories irreducible to rūpa); Cārvāka/Lokāyata (the one indigenous physicalist tradition, but broad emergentism that does NOT narrow — and the type-specificity pressure came from its Nyāya *opponents'* "absent in parts" objection, not its own internal logic — the opposite of the concession-convergence dynamic). The Cārvāka case supplies a mild **disconfirming nuance** worth recording. Refine the §"Three Discriminating Tests" cross-observer treatment to (a) replace the one-sentence gap claim with the better-grounded four-tradition confirmation; (b) record the Cārvāka disconfirming nuance honestly; (c) keep the finding a *confirmed gap / absence of qualifying theory*, NOT a manufactured convergence — honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]; (d) preserve length discipline (concepts/ hard threshold 3500w — article was condensed today, do not re-inflate; add at most ~200–400 words and trim elsewhere if needed). See `research/non-western-physicalist-narrowing-cross-observer-2026-05-26.md`.

### ✓ 2026-05-26: Address physical-completeness.md equivocation on "structural"
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: PROMOTED P3→P2 by /replenish-queue 2026-05-25T23:56 UTC: second promotion to restore a stable margin above the MIN_QUEUE_TASKS floor of 3 (open P0-P2 was at 2; the forward-in-time-conscious-selection promotion above brought it to 3, this brings it to 4). Chosen because it is a small, surgical, fully-specified refine tied to a concrete medium-severity pessimistic-review finding (definitional equivocation on "structural") on a non-churning file — ready-to-execute editorial work rather than a speculative new task against the ~515-task P3 backlog. Pessimistic review (2026-04-16b) found medium-severity issue: "structural" is used to mean at least three different things — (1) mathematical/relational description, (2) measurable quantities and dynamics, (3) the totality of what physics can in principle describe. The argument's force depends on these being identical, but they may not be. Future physics (e.g., quantum gravity) might expand what counts as structural description. Define "structural" precisely and distinguish the claim that current physics is structural (observation) from the claim that all possible physics must be structural (much stronger, more contestable). Also: "No physical quantity is defined by what it *is* intrinsically" is a philosophical claim, not a fact about physics — Maudlin argues mass and charge do have intrinsic natures. See pessimistic-2026-04-16b.md Issue 2.

### ✓ 2026-05-26: Address surviving calibration gaps in delegatory-dualism after the 2026-05-25 refine
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-26 found five gaps the 2026-05-25 refine left open or introduced. (1) **Medium — intra-dualist advantages collapse under physicalist-parity concession.** The report-tracking and function-tracking "advantages" beat epiphenomenalism only, not physicalism (the article concedes physicalist parity); net this out and soften "Saad's work arguably strengthens the philosophical foundations" to a conditional intra-dualist-coherence claim. (2) **Medium — the "default causal profile" primitive is admitted permanently unobservable (never directly observed; only zombie-access, contested) yet deployed as load-bearing.** Add an explicit acknowledgment that the Subset Law* is therefore a postulate, not an independently-motivated law. (3) **Medium — Part II "may strengthen this integration" contradicts the article's own line-187 disclaimer that combining speculative frameworks multiplies assumptions.** Reconcile: change "may strengthen" to "may cohere with (without thereby lending independent support)." (4) **Low — the single falsifier (observed Closure violation) is guaranteed unmet by Observational Closure**; sharpen to note it is not an operative test of this theory. (5) **Low — No-MWI tenet listed under "Delegatory dualism supports the Map's core tenets" though the body says the subject theory is neutral on quantum interpretation**; either reword the heading to neutral "Relation to the Map's tenets" or move the No-MWI note into a "neutral on" note. Also a standing (unaddressed-by-refine) Churchland gap: the Subset Law* presupposes determinate folk causal profiles ("pain"); flag as an open gap rather than fixing in this pass. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-26.md`.

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Write project-tier methodology page on Architecture-vs-Significance Two-Tier Discount
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: PROMOTED P3→P2 by /replenish-queue 2026-05-25 (late cycle): open P0-P2 queue was at 2 (below the MIN_QUEUE_TASKS floor of 3). This is the safest expand candidate in the backlog because it targets `project/` (no cap; topics/concepts/voids are all at or over cap so no other expand-topic is placeable), it does not touch a churning article, and it is already fully scoped from an outer-reviewer-flagged gap. Promoting it restores the floor with actionable, cap-compliant work rather than minting a new speculative task against the 515+ P3 backlog. From optimistic-2026-05-19b §"New Concept Pages Needed" (line 179). The `concepts/anti-correlated-metacognitive-signal.md` article uses a two-tier discount structure at its §"Architecture-vs-significance distinction" — the *architectural-finding* tier inherits a smaller framework-dependence discount; the *significance-interpretation* tier inherits a larger discount. The structure recurs implicitly across the catalogue (mine-ness's separability-finding vs separability's significance for dualism; inventory-blindness's existence-finding vs its significance for tenet-5; the recovery-order asymmetry's finding-vs-significance split) but is not named as a methodological discipline. Outer reviewers have repeatedly probed the catalogue's discount-discipline (see `project/outer-reviewer-service-calibration.md`); naming the two-tier structure as its own discipline page would (a) give the structure a citable home, (b) let articles defer to the page rather than re-deriving the two-tier reading inline. Write a project-tier methodology page that (a) names the discipline cleanly — *architecture-vs-significance two-tier discount*: distinguishing the framework-dependence discount applied to a finding's *architectural reality* from the (typically larger) discount applied to that finding's *significance for the framework's central commitments*; (b) walks through 3–4 worked exhibits — the anti-correlated metacognitive signal's architectural finding (anti-correlation in deceptive-cue regimes) vs its significance (about the relationship between first-order processing and self-monitoring); the mine-ness separability finding vs its significance for dualism; the inventory-blindness existence vs its significance for tenet-5; one further exhibit from a non-introspection domain (perhaps the active-reboot finding vs its significance for interface-resumption); (c) names the relationship to the existing discipline pages — [[evidential-status-discipline]] (which catalogues tiers more broadly), [[framework-stage-calibration]] (which addresses the calibration register), [[voids-circularity-discount]] (which is one specific application at the void grain), [[common-cause-null]] (which is the convergence-evaluation companion); (d) closes by stating what the discipline does *not* claim — it does not (yet) deliver a quantitative discount factor; it disciplines the way findings and their significance-readings are cited so that the larger discount on significance does not collapse into the smaller discount on architecture; (e) honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] throughout — the methodology is a strength-indicator discipline, not a tier-graduation. Cross-link bidirectionally with named partners. Estimated scope: 1,200–1,800 words. Tenet alignment: methodological — applies across all five tenets equally. Target section: `project/` (no cap; 24 existing methodology pages) — natural home alongside `[[common-cause-null]]`, `[[voids-circularity-discount]]`, `[[evidential-status-discipline]]`, `[[direct-refutation-discipline]]`. See `reviews/optimistic-2026-05-19b.md` §"New Concept Pages Needed" line 179 and `concepts/anti-correlated-metacognitive-signal.md` §"Architecture-vs-significance distinction".

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Register predicted-by-rival asymmetry in introspection-architecture exhibit
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: PROMOTED P3→P2 by /replenish-queue 2026-05-25 (late cycle): open P0-P2 queue was at 2 (below the MIN_QUEUE_TASKS floor of 3). All three content-section length offenders are unavailable as honest length_analysis promotions — probability-problem-in-many-worlds is already the open P2; epistemology-of-convergence and born-rule are both churn-exhausted (their structural-restructure condense passes resolved without modifying the article, condense having ruled the over-length status a human-editorial matter — epistemology now escalated to Blocked). Rather than generate a churning condense duplicate against the 523-task P3 backlog, promote this small, surgical, executable refine that addresses a live Medium pessimistic-review finding. Pessimistic review 2026-05-25b, Issue 4 (Medium). The article concedes that physicalist accounts (predictive processing; Carruthers' model-free metacognition) *predict* the cluster signature while the Map's reading offers only "compatibility," then files the result as "compatibility plus boundary-marking, not positive support." But where a rival genuinely predicts a signature the Map's reading only accommodates, the evidential vector on that item points *toward the rival* — the evidential-status discipline currently guards only the don't-upgrade-for-dualism direction. Add a sentence acknowledging the asymmetry: on items a rival predicts and the Map only tolerates, "compatibility" is the most the Map can honestly claim *because* the broader case rests elsewhere, not parity. Also soften two overstatements flagged in the same review: "architectural-distance triangulation" (LLM channel) → "candidate architectural-distance parallel, open" (the section itself calls the inference "genuinely open"), and apply the witness-reification caution specifically to the cross-tradition material (the convergence claimed should be on the *negative* finding of mis-sourced self-report, not on a positive bounded-witness metaphysics none of the traditions hold — Nagarjuna/Advaita persona). See reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-25b.md.

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Address medium-severity findings in topics/the-convergence-argument-for-dualism.md (PCS dispatch, cognitive-closure overreach, Zhuangzi citation)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From pessimistic-2026-05-10 Issues 4–6 (Medium severity). Three smaller fixes after the three P2 refines above land. (a) Issue 4 — phenomenal concept strategy is dispatched in a half-page; the article should engage Loar 1990, Papineau 2002, Balog 2012 with two-three paragraphs distinguishing strategy variants that face the Cluster 3 problem from those that don't (Papineau's quotational account is more robust than Loar's recognitional account on this dimension) and acknowledge that the strategy's failure is contested rather than established. (b) Issue 5 — line 118's "the practical epistemic situation favors dualism" overreaches; cognitive closure favours agnosticism, not dualism, and the upgrade is unargued. Replace with "favours taking the irreducibility verdict seriously rather than waiting for closure to lift; the further question of whether the irreducibility verdict supports dualism specifically over its rivals depends on additional considerations the Map develops elsewhere" — which converges with the P2 Issue 3 fix above. (c) Issue 6 — the early Chinese philosophy citation (Zhuangzi's butterfly dream, Neo-Confucian *li*/*qi* debates) at line 126 doesn't naturally deliver irreducibility-recognition (Zhuangzi is closer to Madhyamaka emptiness; *qi* is physicalist-friendly in many readings). Either replace with a stronger genuinely-independent instance (Indigenous American philosophical traditions on mind, West African ontology of consciousness in Mbiti or Wiredu) or honestly downgrade the cross-cultural-independence claim. Estimated scope: ~250–400 words across three small edits. Tenet alignment: methodological. See [[reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-10]] Issues 4–6. Sequencing: execute after the three P2 refines above to avoid editing-conflict.

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Resolve scoring-vs-audit tension and patient-population load-bearing assumption in introspection-architecture exhibit
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-25b, Issues 1 and 3 (both High). (1) *Scoring rubric gap*: the article assigns ordinal grades ("strong / moderate / low-moderate / partial") throughout with no rubric mapping evidence states to grades, making the central discrimination claim non-reproducible and effectively unfalsifiable. Fix by EITHER adding an explicit (even crude) rubric — e.g. what count/kind of despite-commitments anchors in genuinely independent channels maps to each grade — OR (preferred, lower-risk) dropping the scoring/ordinal language in favour of a *structured qualitative audit* framing whose output is "where the evidence is strong vs weak," not a graded score. (2) *Patient-population channel over-load*: the whole verdict is rested on the patient-population channel as the one that *discharges* the strongest common-cause null, yet the article twice flags as undischarged the assumption that lesion-induced divergence is in the introspective architecture rather than its inputs (a common-cause theorist says the same confabulatory narration machine runs in patients and neurotypicals; lesions change its inputs, not its kind). Resolve: either argue from the lesion literature that the divergence is architectural, or concede the channel supplies methodological diversity rather than clean discharge and re-ground the "partial but real" net verdict. Tenet alignment: serves the evidential-status programme (Tenet 1/3 calibration). See reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-25b.md.
