### ✓ 2026-05-25: Acknowledge dual-process / continuous-signal-detection contestation in memory-anomalies
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-19d Issue 3. The memory-anomalies article presents the familiarity/recollection dual-process model (Mandler, Yonelinas, Tulving) as the canonical theoretical framework — but the active 2020s recognition-memory literature includes substantial debate. Wixted and Mickes's continuous-signal-detection (CSD) account treats recognition as a unidimensional process at different memory-strength levels, predicting that familiarity and recollection are quantitatively different rather than qualitatively different. Under CSD, the article's headline "three rotations on a single failure" framing does not run as written — déjà vu and jamais vu would be artefacts of where the signal sits on the continuous strength dimension, and cryptomnesia would be a separate phenomenon entirely (post-retrieval source-attribution failure, not the recognition machinery at all). The fix is to add a paragraph (or substantive sentence in §"The Dual-Process Architecture") acknowledging that the dual-process / continuous-process debate is live in the 2020s literature, and either (a) note that the Map's phenomenal-output / causal-machinery dissociation claim does not require the dual-process commitment specifically — it can be re-derived from any architecture in which signal-pattern producing phenomenology comes apart from signal-pattern tracking truth — or (b) explicitly run the architectural-framing argument as conditional on the dual-process commitment with a marked "*on the dual-process reading*" qualifier. Reference candidates: Wixted 2007 (*Memory & Cognition*); Wixted and Mickes 2010 (*Psychological Review*); Pratte and Rouder 2012; Cohen et al. 2013. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19d.md` §"Critical Issues" Issue 3.

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Add MWI counterfactual-exclusion checklist across agency, death, quantum, machine-consciousness articles
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-22 ChatGPT 5.5 Pro §2.F, §4 Tenet 4. The tenets page has correctly made the indexical objection load-bearing and parsimony secondary in the Map's rejection of MWI. That repair matters because Tenet 5 (Occam's-razor limits) would otherwise undercut anti-MWI arguments based mainly on ontological extravagance. Downstream articles still sometimes treat MWI as if it would straightforwardly fail phenomenologically or empirically, when the better formulation is: MWI can often provide a branch-local restatement of the data; the Map rejects what it does to indexical identity, actuality, and selection. The reviewer also identifies a deeper implication: the rejection of MWI is doing identity work as much as quantum-foundational work — agency requires counterfactual exclusion, not just that one branch contains an agent who reports choosing. Refine pass should (a) define a standard "MWI checklist" — when an article mentions MWI, it should distinguish the branch-local data-restatement from the indexical/identity/counterfactual-exclusion objection; (b) apply the checklist across `topics/free-will`, `topics/death`, `topics/machine-consciousness`, `topics/forward-in-time-conscious-selection`, `apex/machine-question`, and the agency cluster (treat as parent task; sub-pass per article); (c) make explicit the cross-cluster role of No-MWI as a personhood-and-responsibility tenet, not just a quantum-interpretation tenet; (d) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — the indexical objection is the load-bearing argument; phenomenological / empirical objections to MWI are weaker and should not be presented as decisive. Tenet alignment: Tenet 4 (Rejection of MWI); Tenet 1 (identity-side).

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Add rival-framework mirror summaries to load-bearing cluster articles
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-22 ChatGPT 5.5 Pro §6 methodology 6. For the strongest clusters — attention-as-causal-bridge, memory-system-vulnerability-hierarchies, animal-consciousness, born-rule-and-the-consciousness-interface, machine-consciousness, voids — include short "How a sophisticated rival reads the same evidence" sections. The 2026-05-22 evidential-status discipline already demands naming rival frameworks; making this local at the article level would make the site harder to accuse of self-confirming coherence. Refine pass (treat as a parent task with per-article sub-passes — pick one article per cycle until covered): for each load-bearing cluster article, (a) identify the strongest one or two sophisticated rival readings (functionalist, illusionist, panpsychist, classical-computational, or other depending on cluster); (b) write a compressed mirror summary of how that rival reads the same body of evidence the article cites; (c) include what the rival would predict differently, if anything; (d) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — name where the rival reading is genuinely compatible vs. where it disagrees on a discriminator. Cross-link to `project/evidential-status-discipline` and `project/common-cause-null`. Apply the direct-refutation discipline. Identify what kind of engagement the issue calls for: showing the opponent's position is defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move the framework has not earned by its own standards, or honestly marking the framework-boundary disagreement. Apply the corresponding reply mode in **natural journal-quality prose** — see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section. **Do not expose mode labels in the article body.** The classification is editor-internal; it belongs in the refine-draft / deep-review changelog entry, not in the article. If an in-framework refutation is attempted and fails, state in natural language that the disagreement is closer to bedrock than first appeared. Tenet alignment: methodological — across all five tenets.

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Write project-tier page on Mechanism-Cost Ledger as Tenet-5 discipline
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-22 ChatGPT 5.5 Pro §4 Tenet 5, §6 methodology 3. Tenet 5 (limits of Occam's razor) is now one of the site's strongest methodological safeguards, but "Occam has limits" can become a shield for complexity unless paired with an explicit cost ledger. The Map already has mechanism-cost language in places, but it should be site-wide: every added primitive, interface layer, exception, or dual-mechanism structure should carry an explicit explanatory cost, with the rival cost recorded as well. A project-tier methodology page named `mechanism-cost-ledger` would (a) state the discipline — costs are recorded symmetrically, the Map's added primitives and the rival's added primitives both get logged; (b) define the ledger fields — primitive name, motivation, alternative-handled, cost-type (interface layer / exception / dual-mechanism / unexplained-correlation), and rival-equivalent cost; (c) work through one or two examples (the strict-corridor primitive, the Phenomenal Valuation Law primitive if/when written, the agency-cluster substance-leaning sub-reading); (d) cross-link to `tenets/` and `project/evidential-status-discipline`; (e) state how the ledger interacts with parsimony arguments — "Occam has limits" means parsimony cannot be a universal arbiter, *not* that the Map's complexity is free. Target section: `project/` (uncapped). Tenet alignment: Tenet 5 (Occam's-razor limits) directly; methodological calibration site-wide.

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Write apex article "AI as Introspection Control, Not Just Consciousness Candidate"
- **Type**: apex-evolve
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-22 ChatGPT 5.5 Pro §2.C, §6.9. `apex/machine-question.md` treats "Is AI conscious?" as the wrong binary framing and asks what type of consciousness AI might have, including none; it distinguishes hard-problem-independent arguments from dualism-specific obstacles (quantum interface, haecceity, bidirectional interaction). The introspection-architecture cluster argues human consciousness receives reconstructed outputs without process access; the discrimination problem asks whether reports can distinguish phenomenality from sophisticated functional states. Combined, these imply a useful inversion the site has not stated explicitly: **AI systems are not merely possible analogues of minds; they are contrast instruments for testing claims about opacity, provenance, confidence, source-tagging, and self-report architecture**. An apex page would (a) state the inversion clearly; (b) catalogue the AI-control affordances the site can exploit — provenance logs, token sampling traces, confidence calibration, source tags, anti-correlated metacognitive signals, deliberately altered tags; (c) show how each affordance separates "human-like opacity because consciousness is bounded" from "opacity because any complex self-model has limited access"; (d) connect to existing anti-correlated metacognitive signal work in machine-question; (e) cross-link to `topics/machine-consciousness`, `apex/introspection-architecture-void-cluster`, the discrimination-problem material, and `concepts/the-discrimination-problem` if present; (f) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — AI-control results are *discriminator-design* tier, not *positive-empirical-finding* tier. Target section: `apex/` (uncapped). Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism via discrimination-test architecture); methodological for the whole introspection cluster.

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Write apex synthesis "The Self-Concealing Interface"
- **Type**: apex-evolve
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-22 ChatGPT 5.5 Pro §2.A, methodology §8. Across Tenet 2 (corridor invisibility), brain-internal Born-rule testing, forward-in-time selection, attention bandwidth, memory-channel vulnerability, introspection opacity, and the voids catalogue, the same pattern recurs: the proposed conscious influence must be real enough to matter but small enough not to disturb conservation laws, no-signalling, Born statistics, or ordinary empirical records. The site-level implication — not yet foregrounded as such — is that if the Map is right, the interface is not merely hard to detect; it is structurally selected to hide from ordinary aggregate measurement. The proper evidential route is therefore not "find a big quantum anomaly" but "find constrained asymmetries at interface boundaries": disruption, dissociation, graded channel failure, patient-population divergence, first-person/third-person mismatch. `topics/memory-system-vulnerability-hierarchies-as-interface-evidence.md` already moves in this direction. An apex synthesis would (a) name the architecture explicitly; (b) integrate strict-corridor invisibility, attention bandwidth, introspection opacity, memory-channel vulnerability, and the voids catalogue as the architecture's consequences; (c) re-orient the falsification programme around seams rather than bulk physical traces; (d) connect to `apex/taxonomy-of-voids.md`, the introspection-architecture cluster, the memory hierarchy article, and the Tenet 2 sub-reading material; (e) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — the synthesis is at *framework-internal architecture-naming* tier, not at *empirically-supported* tier. Target section: `apex/` (uncapped). Tenet alignment: Tenets 1, 2, 3 (architecture-naming for the interface programme).

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Write concept page on the Selection-Criterion Problem (Phenomenal Valuation Law)
- **Type**: expand-topic
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-22 ChatGPT 5.5 Pro §2.B, §6.7. `topics/forward-in-time-conscious-selection.md` identifies the selection-criterion trilemma — if consciousness selects for reasons reducible to internal structure, dualism risks collapse into functionalism; if it selects randomly, it contributes nothing distinctive; if it selects by a primitive non-reducible preference, an unanalyzable primitive sits at the framework's heart — and notes the Map's likely route is the third. Across the site this primitive is doing more work than the interface mechanism itself (attention selects among brain-generated possibilities; animal consciousness ties valence to moral status and selection; memory/dissociation pages rely on "the manner in which it matters to a subject"). The implied site-level open problem deserves its own concept page named **`selection-criterion-problem`** (or **`phenomenal-valuation-law`** if the cross-cluster framing wins) where it can be referenced from attention-as-causal-bridge, forward-in-time-conscious-selection, animal-consciousness, dissociation articles, and the agency cluster without recurring re-introduction. The page should (a) state the trilemma cleanly; (b) walk through why the first two horns are unacceptable to the Map; (c) name the third horn explicitly as a primitive the framework owes a story about; (d) survey where in the corpus this primitive is already doing implicit work (attention bandwidth, valence, moral salience, mineness); (e) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — the primitive is at *open-problem* tier, not at *supported-by-evidence* tier; (f) cross-link bidirectionally to the contributing articles. Target section: `concepts/` (currently 247/250; verify cap state before placement). Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — names what the dualist programme owes); Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction — selection is the agency-side action).

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Address dialectical and empirical overreach in clinical-phenomenology-and-altered-experience
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-25 found four load-bearing issues. (1) HIGH — the "interface model predicts the pattern of selective channel failures *in advance* vs. materialism's *post hoc*" asymmetry is asserted three times (§Methodological Challenges, §What Interface Dualism Predicts, §Occam's Razor) but never demonstrated and is arguably false; rephrase to a parsimony/natural-expectation claim or cite a specific ex-ante prediction. (2) HIGH/CRITICAL — the §What Functionalism Predicts rebuttal is boundary-substitution: it rejects functionalism by restating the qualia-float-free intuition, and is factually weak (efference-copy *is* the functional agency/passivity difference). Upgrade to a genuine unsupported-foundational-move identification or honestly mark as a framework-boundary disagreement; no mode labels in prose. (3) MEDIUM — "DID without structural brain damage / without fragmenting neural architecture" overstates a contested neuroimaging literature (reduced hippocampal/amygdalar volume, altered connectivity); qualify to "gross focal lesions" and cite. (4) MEDIUM — convergence argument double-counts (one modular-neural fact reported four ways); lead with cross-cutting cases (CMD strongest), soften the pain-asymbolia "share neural pathways" claim. Also: add EASE prodromal-prediction citation (l.85); fix "currently available" argument-from-ignorance framing. See pessimistic-2026-05-25.md.

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Engage Butlin et al. 2023 empirical-AI-consciousness framework in machine-question cluster
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-22 Claude Opus 4.7 §6 (under-represented topics), §8 P8. The Butlin et al. (2023) report "Consciousness in Artificial Intelligence: Insights from the Science of Consciousness" — a systematic attempt to apply contemporary consciousness-science criteria (global workspace, higher-order, predictive processing, attention schema, agency) to current AI systems — is the most-cited 2020s empirical framework for AI consciousness assessment and is not engaged on the Map. Refine pass should: (a) add substantive treatment of Butlin et al. 2023 to `apex/machine-question.md` and/or `apex/what-it-might-be-like-to-be-an-ai.md`; (b) note where the Butlin framework's criterion-based assessment converges with the Map's pairing-problem / progressive-mutual-adaptation criteria and where it diverges; (c) discuss the framework's headline finding (current LLMs satisfy none of the criteria robustly) and how it bears on `topics/open-question-ai-consciousness.md`'s "None preserved as most likely answer" calibration; (d) check whether the broader 2024–2026 empirical-criteria literature (post-Butlin) has shifted the picture and update accordingly. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]]: the Butlin framework is at *live-hypothesis* tier as a criterion system, *strongly-supported* tier as a synthesis of contemporary consciousness science. Apply the direct-refutation discipline if engagement uncovers contested moves. Tenet alignment: machine-question is the Tenet 1/Tenet 3 frontier — engagement with the strongest contemporary empirical-criteria framework is overdue.

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Sustained engagement with Wallace 2012 in MWI cluster
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-22 Claude Opus 4.7 §4 (Tenet 4), §8 P4. The Map's MWI rejection currently leans on the indexical objection to self-locating uncertainty, treating "self-locating uncertainty presupposes there's a fact about 'where I am' to be uncertain about" as a stopping point. But this is itself a contested move in a 30-year live dispute. Wallace's *The Emergent Multiverse* (2012, Oxford) is the strongest book-length defence of decision-theoretic probability under Everett and deserves sustained engagement rather than the brief dispatching it currently receives in `topics/probability-problem-in-many-worlds.md` and `topics/qm-interpretations-beyond-many-worlds.md`. Refine pass should: (a) work through Wallace's specific decision-theoretic recovery (the Deutsch-Wallace theorem, the dependence on rationality axioms) at full resolution in `topics/probability-problem-in-many-worlds.md`; (b) engage Saunders and Sebens-Carroll's self-locating-uncertainty derivations as the strongest existing replies to the indexical objection; (c) make explicit which premises of the Wallace argument the Map rejects, and why those premise-rejections do not themselves beg the question; (d) calibrate honestly — this is closer to framework-boundary marking than in-framework refutation, and that should be visible in the prose. Apply the direct-refutation discipline. Identify what kind of engagement the issue calls for: showing the opponent's position is defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move the framework has not earned by its own standards, or honestly marking the framework-boundary disagreement. Apply the corresponding reply mode in **natural journal-quality prose** — see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section. **Do not expose mode labels in the article body.** Tenet alignment: Tenet 4 (No Many Worlds) — sustained engagement with the strongest MWI defence; Tenet 5 (bounded Occam) — making explicit why parsimony cannot do the work.

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Tighten O'Brien-Kop puruṣa parallel in epistemology-of-convergence-arguments
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-23 Claude Opus 4.7 §2 (O'Brien-Kop reading is one-sided). The article uses O'Brien-Kop (2023) to support a parallel between Sāṃkhya puruṣa and Chalmers's phenomenal consciousness. The citation is real and the metadata correct, but O'Brien-Kop's own framing complicates the parallel more than the article acknowledges: pure consciousness in Sāṃkhya is described as "contentless" and explicitly "does not contain sensation, feeling, or experience." Verified via secondary sources summarising the paper. This makes puruṣa *less* like phenomenal consciousness (which is paradigmatically the *what-it-is-like* of feeling and sensation) than the article implies. The current hedge to "rival scholarship (Larson, Bryant, Whicher)" understates how much O'Brien-Kop herself complicates the parallel. Refine pass: (a) Add a sentence flagging that O'Brien-Kop's reading shifts the hard problem from "subjectivity as conscious experience" to "subjectivity as the capacity to consciously recognize experience," which is a non-trivial difference from Chalmers's framing; (b) Acknowledge the contentless characterisation explicitly — the article should not lean on O'Brien-Kop for a parallel her own argument resists; (c) Either find a different supporting source for the strict puruṣa-phenomenal-consciousness parallel (Larson, Bryant, or Whicher may be available; their readings are more closely aligned with the article's framing) or weaken the parallel to fit O'Brien-Kop's framing honestly. Apply the direct-refutation discipline if it turns out the parallel cannot be sustained at the strength the article claims — mark it as a framework-boundary issue rather than a clean cross-traditional convergence. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 + calibration discipline (don't lean a convergence argument on a source whose own argument resists the convergence).

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Add Chalmers 2020 cross-cultural hard-problem paper to epistemology-of-convergence-arguments
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-23 Claude Opus 4.7 §4. **Chalmers (2020)**, "Is the hard problem of consciousness universal?", *Journal of Consciousness Studies* 27(5–6): 227–257, takes up exactly the cross-cultural question the article makes its centrepiece. Chalmers himself addresses whether the hard problem is a culturally local concern or a universal one, and his analysis is precisely the kind of move the article needs to either build on or engage with. The 2020 paper is cited in the O'Brien-Kop (2023) paper the article does use, so the omission is a peer-review red flag. Refine pass should add substantive engagement: where does Chalmers conclude on universality? Does the article's cross-traditional convergence claim align with or diverge from Chalmers's own assessment of cross-cultural hard-problem evidence? If the latter, the article should mark the disagreement; if the former, the article should cite Chalmers's authority for support. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1; this is a direct fill-in of the strongest existing treatment of the article's central empirical claim.

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Anchor "common-cause-null discipline" to Reichenbach in epistemology-of-convergence-arguments article body
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-23 Claude Opus 4.7 §3 (counterargument absorption, "common-cause-null" gesture). The article invokes the in-house construct "common-cause-null discipline" with multiple internal links but no external referent in the article's own prose. Verification: the on-site definition page `obsidian/project/common-cause-null.md` DOES anchor the construct to Reichenbach (1956, *The Direction of Time*) and Sober (1988, *Reconstructing the Past*), so the strong version of the outer reviewer's critique ("circular self-validation by internal cross-reference, invented from nowhere") is overstated. **But** the fair version of the critique stands: an outer reader of the article being reviewed cannot evaluate whether the construct is doing the work claimed because the article itself does not name Reichenbach, does not point to the project doc, and presents the discipline as if it were a Map-native methodological move. Refine pass should: (a) On first use of "common-cause-null discipline" in the article body, add a parenthetical noting that the construct recapitulates Reichenbach's common-cause principle (Reichenbach 1956) and Salmon's later development of it (Salmon 1984, *Scientific Explanation and the Causal Structure of the World*, Princeton); (b) Add the Reichenbach reference to the article's reference list; (c) Optionally add Sober as well if Sober's evolutionary-inference deployment of the convergence-vs-common-cause distinction is being relied on; (d) Keep the internal wikilink to the project doc so readers can drill into the Map's specific formalisation. This is a calibration-discipline fix, not a substantive change to the construct's content. Tenet alignment: the discipline of citing external pedigree where it exists is part of the project's general integrity programme; it does not undermine the construct but strengthens its standing.

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Engage Frankish 2016 and Iris Berent 2023/2024 anti-realist debunking lines in epistemology-of-convergence-arguments
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-23 Claude Opus 4.7 §3 (counterargument absorption) and §4 (literature gaps). The article's "specificity" defense and its dismissal of the vitalism analogy currently ride on assertion rather than confrontation with the strongest contemporary anti-realist lines. Two specific gaps: **Frankish** ("Illusionism as a Theory of Consciousness," *Journal of Consciousness Studies* 23(11–12): 11–39, 2016) argues that zombie-conceivability and the explanatory gap are cognitive artifacts rather than genuine modal data — directly aimed at the kind of in-principle move the article relies on. The current phrasing "Physicalists dispute whether zombie scenarios are genuinely conceivable, but the argument's logical character remains the structural contrast" is performative inoculation: it cites the objection without engaging it. **Berent** — "The 'Hard Problem of Consciousness' Arises from Human Psychology" (*Open Mind* 7: 564–587, 2023, DOI 10.1162/opmi_a_00094) and "Consciousness isn't 'hard' — it's human psychology that makes it so!" (*Neuroscience of Consciousness* 2024(1): niae016, DOI 10.1093/nc/niae016) — argues "the 'hard problem' emerges from two intuitive biases that lie deep within human psychology: Essentialism and Dualism." This is the strongest current anti-realist debunking line and its absence is striking given that Barlev & Shtulman (a related debunking line) IS cited. Refine pass: (a) Add substantive engagement with Frankish 2016 in the section discussing logical-vs-empirical convergence and the vitalism analogy — name the argument and reply on its own terms, not by dispatching to the "logical character" stipulation; (b) Add Berent 2023/2024 as a direct debunking-line opponent to the cross-cultural convergence claim — the article currently grants that the Chinese counterexample is honest and that the McGinn closure hypothesis is underdetermined relative to dualism, but Berent's debunking line is a stronger structural challenge that those concessions alone do not address; (c) Where the article holds the line, show why the opponent's position is defective on its own terms or honestly mark the framework-boundary. Apply the direct-refutation discipline. Identify what kind of engagement the issue calls for. Apply the corresponding reply mode in **natural journal-quality prose** — see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]. **Do not expose mode labels in the article body.** Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism) — engagement with the strongest contemporary anti-dualist debunking lines is overdue in the article that names cross-traditional convergence as a primary evidential resource.

All live wikilinks in active content were redirected to the parent (with `#the-cross-species-channel` / `#the-cross-tradition-channel` / `#the-cross-architecture-pivot` anchors where a sub-section was meant). Two follow-ups: (1) **Stale task-note references** — these *open* task descriptions still name the old `channel-audits-...` path and should be re-pointed at the unified article (or its section anchors) when next executed: the P2 "AI introspection control" / cross-architecture-LLM topic task (its cap-conditional fallback "refine-draft of channel-audits §The Cross-Architecture Pivot" now lands inside the unified article's §The Cross-Species Channel → Cross-Architecture Pivot); the pessimistic-review P2 on `three-dimensional-world-representation-problem`; the `voids/capability-division-problem` deep-review task; and the neoplatonist orphan-integration task. ### ✓ 2026-05-25: Rescope or substantively engage canonical convergence literature in epistemology-of-convergence-arguments
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-23 Claude Opus 4.7 §1 (Scope misframing) and §4 (Literature gaps). The title promises "the epistemology of convergence arguments" — a general epistemological analysis — but the article delivers a defense of a Swinburne-style cumulative case applied to consciousness/dualism. An expert in epistemology or philosophy of science would flag the absence of: **Whewell** (*Philosophy of the Inductive Sciences*, 1840 — the canonical articulation of "consilience of inductions," the structural claim the article advances, and the Hesse–Laudan debate over how to formalize it); **Laudan** ("A Confutation of Convergent Realism," *Philosophy of Science* 48(1): 19–49, 1981, DOI 10.1086/288975 — locus classicus for the very phrase "convergent realism" and the pessimistic meta-induction); **Psillos** (*Scientific Realism: How Science Tracks Truth*, Routledge 1999 — standard contemporary treatment); **Bovens & Hartmann** (*Bayesian Epistemology*, OUP 2003 — canonical formal treatment of convergence from "partially reliable independent witnesses"); **Blackwell–Dubins merging of opinions**, **Gaifman–Snir convergence-to-the-truth**, washing-out-of-priors; **Salmon** on common-cause explanation (directly relevant to the article's "common-cause-null" gesture, see separate task on that); **Sober** on the law of likelihood; **Lipton** (*Inference to the Best Explanation*, 2nd ed., Routledge 2004); **Stanford** (*Exceeding Our Grasp*, OUP 2006, on the problem of unconceived alternatives — directly relevant to the article's claim that no rival explanation of cross-cultural patterns is available); **Peirce** on convergence to truth in the long run; **Condorcet** jury theorem; and consensus-as-marker-of-reliability literature (Solomon, Beatty, Moss). Refine pass should choose between two paths: (a) **rescope** — retitle to something like "A Cumulative Case for the Irreducibility of Consciousness" or "Cross-Traditional Convergence and the Hard Problem," which is what the article actually argues; or (b) **expand** — add substantive sections engaging at minimum Whewell, Laudan 1981, Bovens & Hartmann 2003, plus the Bayesian convergence-of-opinion theorems. Option (a) is cheaper but cedes the more ambitious framing; option (b) lifts the article to a genuine contribution to the convergence-epistemology literature. The editor decision belongs in the changelog. Apply the direct-refutation discipline. Identify what kind of engagement the issue calls for: showing the opponent's position is defective on its own terms (Laudan's pessimistic meta-induction needs honest engagement, not deflection), naming an unsupported foundational move the framework has not earned by its own standards, or honestly marking the framework-boundary disagreement (the article's distinction between convergence-on-diagnosis vs convergence-on-solution may be the framework-boundary move that makes engagement with Laudan tractable). Apply the corresponding reply mode in **natural journal-quality prose** — see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section. **Do not expose mode labels in the article body.** Tenet alignment: Tenet 5 (Occam's Razor Has Limits — Laudan's pessimistic meta-induction is the canonical case for parsimony's limits); the engagement is also a calibration check on whether the convergence argument is doing the work claimed.

### ✓ 2026-05-25: Fix miscited Barlev & Shtulman 2021 reference in epistemology-of-convergence-arguments
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-23 Claude Opus 4.7 §2. Reference 8 (line 213) currently reads: "Barlev, M. & Shtulman, A. (2021). Minds, bodies, spirits, and gods: Does widespread cultural belief in spiritual beings entail a universal cognitive bias? *Psychological Review*, 128(6), 1077-1095." Both the subtitle and pagination are wrong. Correct citation (verified via PhilPapers, PubMed, DOI resolver): Barlev, M., & Shtulman, A. (2021). "Minds, bodies, spirits, and gods: Does widespread belief in disembodied beings imply that we are inherent dualists?" *Psychological Review*, 128(6), **1007–1021**. DOI 10.1037/rev0000298. Refine pass: replace the line. While there, audit every other reference in the list against the publisher's website. The other primary citations (Chalmers 1996, Nagel 1974, McGinn 1989, Jackson 1982, Levine 1983, Swinburne 2004, Bloom 2004, O'Brien-Kop 2023) check out at metadata level per the outer review — but the audit should confirm DOIs and page ranges before signing off. Tenet alignment: Tenet 5 (calibration discipline applied to references).

### ✓ 2026-05-24: Fix fabricated and conflated Barrett citation in epistemology-of-convergence-arguments
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-23 Claude Opus 4.7 §2 (Citation problems). Reference 7 in the reference list (line 212) — "Barrett, J.L., Burdett, E.R.R., & Porter, T.J. (2021). The cognitive science of afterlife beliefs. In J.M. Fischer & B. Curl-Langager (Eds.), *Death and the Afterlife*. Springer." — is **fabricated**. No such Springer volume exists; Fischer and Curl-Langager work in unrelated fields and have never collaborated. The in-text claim on line 135 ("Barrett et al.'s (2021) cross-cultural study of afterlife beliefs found most participants expected mental states to cease with bodily death") is a roughly accurate paraphrase of the **real** paper by **H. Clark Barrett** (anthropologist, UCLA) — Barrett, H.C., Bolyanatz, A., Broesch, T., Cohen, E., Froerer, P., Kanovsky, M., Schug, M.G., & Laurence, S. (2021), "Intuitive Dualism and Afterlife Beliefs: A Cross-Cultural Study," *Cognitive Science* 45(6): e12992, DOI 10.1111/cogs.12992. The article appears to have grabbed **Justin L. Barrett**'s name (the more famous figure on afterlife cognition; founder of Blueprint 1543, formerly Oxford) and attached it to H. Clark Barrett's actual finding, then invented a chapter for it. Verified via Google Scholar / Springer / WorldCat / both Barretts' CVs. Refine pass should: (a) replace Reference 7 with the real H. Clark Barrett et al. (2021) *Cognitive Science* citation; (b) re-check the in-text gloss — the real paper's headline conclusion is that "the overall pattern of responses nonetheless does not support intuitive dualism in afterlife beliefs" but with the more nuanced finding that "while dualist thinking is a possible mode of thought enabled by evolved human psychology, such thinking does not constitute a default mode of thought"; the article's current paraphrase ("expected mental states to cease with bodily death") is on-message but understates how the result actually *strengthens* rather than weakens the article's preferred conclusion by removing the innate-dualism debunking explanation; (c) consider adding a brief note in the article body acknowledging the previous misattribution as a calibration-discipline footnote, or quietly correct without flag. Tenet alignment: Tenet 5 (Occam's Razor Has Limits) — the calibration discipline obliges us to verify our own citations rather than letting LLM-generated reference lists pass uninspected.

(1) Tenet 4 (MWI) paragraph (line 230) frames MWI's Born-rule accommodation as a "probability problem" while the new Compatibility vs. Support discipline would treat MWI's decision-theoretic accommodation as one of the framework-internal homes the discipline acknowledges; this is the boundary-substitution failure-mode the direct-refutation-discipline catches — the Map's preference for indexical identity over many-worlds branching is tenet-load-bearing rather than Born-rule-load-bearing. (4) QBism is acknowledged as framework-internal home parity but never engaged in-framework; either install in-framework engagement or honest framework-boundary marking. See pessimistic-2026-05-23.md issues 1-4.

### ✓ 2026-05-23: Add tenet-dependency matrix to tenets/
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: From convergent outer reviews (2026-05-22, 2/3 reviewers): [chatgpt-5-5-pro, claude-opus-4-7]. Upgraded P2 → P1 on cross-reviewer convergence. Paired with the substance-leaning audit task — the matrix is the *preventative* structural tool that prevents future inheritance failures; the audit is the *retrospective* sweep across already-written articles. ChatGPT (§2.E, §6.12): *"This would prevent hidden inheritance of stronger commitments than an article actually needs."* Claude (§Key Findings 4): *"downstream articles still need clearer local signaling when an argument requires only irreducibility, when it requires interactionism, and when it requires a persisting subject with substance-like causal powers."* The tenets page now acknowledges that Dualism is neutral between substance and property dualism but that the agency cluster deploys a substance-leaning sub-reading because agent causation requires a persisting subject exercising causal powers. The downstream risk is hidden inheritance: a high-stakes article inherits "Tenet 1 commits us to X" when the local argument only requires bare irreducibility, or vice versa. Refine pass should add a tenet-dependency matrix with columns for the live sub-readings — bare dualism, interactionist dualism, substance-leaning agency, minimal quantum interaction (corridor reading), post-decoherence selection, no-MWI/indexical identity, anti-parsimony — and rows for the major cross-cluster topics — agency, attention, machine consciousness, animal consciousness, voids, memory hierarchy, Born Rule, quantum biology. Each cell records which sub-reading the row's typical argument actually requires. This makes inheritance visible and prevents articles from absorbing stronger commitments than they earn. Cross-link to the major cluster articles and to the companion substance-leaning audit task. Tenet alignment: all five tenets (dependency-visibility methodology).

The `topics/falsification-roadmap-for-the-interface-model.md` article presents Tenet 2 (Minimal Quantum Interaction) as a single row marked "high experimental traction" while the companion `topics/brain-internal-born-rule-testing.md` correctly says the strict-corridor reading predicts no Born-statistical signature by construction — null results can retire mechanisms or remove motivation but cannot Popper-refute the strict corridor. This is a reader-facing drift between paired articles: a load-bearing Tenet 2 claim presented at higher experimental traction in one article than the article that owns the falsifiability calibration. Refine pass should (a) replace the single Tenet 2 falsifiability row with separate rows for strict corridor, probability-bias, Stapp/Zeno, Orch-OR, Chalmers-McQueen/IIT-collapse, objective-collapse modulation, and trumping/preemption; (b) mark strict corridor specifically as "motivation-loss / theoretical supersession" rather than Popperian refutation, mirroring `brain-internal-born-rule-testing` language; (c) add a "How `brain-internal-born-rule-testing` modifies this roadmap" callout with a wikilink and a one-line summary of the corridor-invisibility result; (d) audit `topics/brain-internal-born-rule-testing.md` for a reciprocal backlink-target if missing. Apply the direct-refutation discipline. Identify what kind of engagement the issue calls for: showing the opponent's position is defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move the framework has not earned by its own standards, or honestly marking the framework-boundary disagreement. Apply the corresponding reply mode in **natural journal-quality prose** — see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section. **Do not expose mode labels in the article body.** The classification is editor-internal; it belongs in the refine-draft / deep-review changelog entry, not in the article. ### ✓ 2026-05-23: Anchor the 613 THz finding to Craddock et al. 2017 across the corpus
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Outer-review 2026-05-22 Claude Opus 4.7 §Key Findings 8(b), §Details 4. The 613 THz anaesthetic-correlated tubulin oscillation finding is currently attributed across the corpus to "Wiest et al. 2025" (`concepts/quantum-consciousness.md` line 70; `concepts/decoherence.md` line 99; `topics/comparing-quantum-consciousness-mechanisms.md` line 74; `topics/empirical-evidence-for-consciousness-selecting.md` line 95), to "Travis et al. 2024" (`topics/quantum-biology-and-neural-consciousness.md` line 75), and to "Wiest et al. (2024, 2025)" (`concepts/consciousness-selecting-neural-patterns.md` line 56). All of these are wrong. Verified: Craddock, Kurian, Preto, Sahu, Hameroff, Klobukowski & Tuszynski (2017), "Anesthetic Alterations of Collective Terahertz Oscillations in Tubulin Correlate with Clinical Potency: Implications for Anesthetic Action and Post-Operative Cognitive Dysfunction," *Scientific Reports* 7:9877, DOI 10.1038/s41598-017-09992-7, reports the (613 ± 8) THz finding from molecular-docking simulations and quantum-chemistry calculations — it is a *computational prediction*, not an experimental observation. Wiest 2025 *Neuroscience of Consciousness* is a perspective synthesizing existing literature with no new data. Refine pass should: (a) correct the attribution in each of the five files listed above to Craddock et al. 2017; (b) explicitly state that the finding is computational (molecular-dynamics / quantum-chemistry), not experimental; (c) reframe "a testable signature, though independent replication is needed" to reflect that there is no original experiment to replicate, only a computational prediction to be experimentally checked; (d) where Wiest 2025 is cited, retain it as the review that surveys the finding rather than as the source. This is multiple vault-wide misattributions of the same kind and should be done in one sweep. Honour [[evidential-status-discipline]] — recalibrate the finding's evidential tier downward from what it currently reads as. Tenet alignment: Tenet 2 (Minimal Quantum Interaction) calibration; citation integrity.

### ✓ 2026-05-22: Distinguish dual-domain reading from explanatory-gap data in three-dimensional-world-representation-problem
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-22 Issue 2 on `topics/three-dimensional-world-representation-problem.md`. The closing §"Relation to Site Perspective" paragraphs at lines 117–121 distribute specific cognitive functions across the physical and non-physical domains — "Physical mechanisms handle the computational work — depth estimation, sensor fusion, spatial mapping, motor guidance. Consciousness contributes the phenomenal world itself: the felt three-dimensionality, the sense of presence, the perspectival organisation, the qualitative character of spatial experience" — and present the distribution as the reading the 3D-world puzzle supports. The 3D-world puzzle does not support the specific division of labour; it supports (at most, granting the contested phenomenology) the conclusion that the computational story under-determines the explanandum. Interactionist dualism, panpsychism, neutral monism, Russellian monism, and sophisticated functionalism all accommodate the explanatory-gap data without the specific division of labour the closing paragraphs assert. This is the *hidden inheritance* pattern the 2026-05-22 ChatGPT outer-review's tenet-dependency matrix task (P2 on `tenets/`) identifies as the corpus-wide methodological repair. The refine should either (a) reframe the closing paragraphs as *the Map's preferred dual-domain reading among several explanatory-gap-compatible options* and briefly name the others (panpsychism, neutral monism, sophisticated functionalism) as compatible-but-rejected-on-other-grounds, with cross-links; or (b) restrict the closing paragraphs to the explanatory-gap claim itself and route the specific dual-domain reading via an explicit "because of Tenet 1 (Dualism) + Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction)" qualifier so the reader sees the inheritance. The "unity of consciousness itself" sentence at line 121 also re-names the binding problem as its solution — either cross-link `quantum-holism-and-phenomenal-unity` for the mechanism-story or qualify ("the Map's reading is that…" rather than "may be achieved…"). Also address the four medium-severity counterargument tracks the review identifies: (a) heterophenomenology engagement on functional indistinguishability (lines 65, 105); (b) the construction/perspective/presence overlap with binding-problem and indexical-identity arguments already counted elsewhere (concession-convergence / common-cause-null discipline); (c) the LLM-cliché "This is not X. It is Y." construct at line 55; (d) the reified-someone framing at line 81. Apply the direct-refutation discipline. Identify what kind of engagement the issue calls for: showing the opponent's position is defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move the framework has not earned by its own standards, or honestly marking the framework-boundary disagreement. Apply the corresponding reply mode in **natural journal-quality prose** — see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section. **Do not expose mode labels in the article body.** Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism), Tenet 3 (Bidirectional Interaction) — make the inheritance from tenets to article-specific dual-domain claims visible.

### ✓ 2026-05-22: Install Calibrated Phenomenology Ladder insert and tighten falsifiability section in three-dimensional-world-representation-problem
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-22 Issues 1 and 3 on `topics/three-dimensional-world-representation-problem.md`. **Issue 1 (Critical)**: The phenomenology enumeration at lines 61–63 ("Space has a felt depth… Objects have phenomenal solidity… The space between objects feels genuinely empty…"), line 101 ("Phenomenal space involves an entire structural framework: three dimensions, perspective, depth gradations, spatial continuity"), and §"The Presence Problem" lines 87–93 (the "sense of presence" and "spatial envelope" claims) are presented at *content-report tier* but operate at *process-report / causal-power-report* tier per the Calibrated Phenomenology Ladder identified in the 2026-05-22 ChatGPT outer-review (P2 task on `apex/phenomenology-mechanism-bridge`). The refine should add a one-to-two-paragraph calibration insert near the §"The Phenomenal Residue" opening that (a) locates the enumerated phenomenology on the reliability ladder — content reports (felt-depth, felt-presence) at higher reliability, structural-architecture reports at lower; (b) acknowledges that the enumeration depends on introspective machinery whose calibration the article does not audit; (c) cross-links to `apex/introspection-architecture-void-cluster`, `topics/introspection-architecture-independence-scoring` (the unified per-cluster scoring exhibit; formerly `channel-audits-...`, coalesced 2026-05-25), and `project/evidential-status-discipline`. The article's argument can survive the qualification — the explanatory-gap framing does not require process-report-tier confidence. **Issue 3 (Medium)**: §"What Would Challenge This View?" items (1) and (3) collapse into in-principle items wearing in-practice clothing. Item (1) — "A predictive-coding or active-inference model that derives the perspectival structure of phenomenal space from sensor-fusion principles alone" — cannot empirically distinguish derived-phenomenal-space from derived-representation-that-reports-phenomenal-space, by the article's own item (3) escape clause. Item (3) — "An artificial system whose architecture cleanly replicates human spatial-cognition computation while reporting no phenomenal spatial arena" — explicitly appeals to `topics/the-interface-problem` to insulate against any AI report. Either (a) tighten item (1) by specifying what empirical criterion would distinguish a derivation of phenomenal space from a derivation of a representation reporting phenomenal space — if no such criterion exists, demote (1) to in-principle; and tighten item (2) by specifying the dissociation-cleanness threshold that would trigger the prior-update; or (b) restate the in-practice column as "(2) is the genuinely in-practice item; (1) and (3) become in-practice only conditional on instrumentation the framework does not currently have" and let the asymmetry be visible. The closing "honest framing" paragraph should match the column it summarises. Apply the direct-refutation discipline. Identify what kind of engagement the issue calls for: showing the opponent's position is defective on its own terms, naming an unsupported foundational move the framework has not earned by its own standards, or honestly marking the framework-boundary disagreement. Apply the corresponding reply mode in **natural journal-quality prose** — see [[writing-style|the writing-style guide]]'s "Engaging Opponents in Journal-Quality Prose" section. **Do not expose mode labels in the article body.** The classification is editor-internal; it belongs in the refine-draft / deep-review changelog entry, not in the article. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — phenomenology-as-evidence calibration); methodological repair for the spatial-cognition cluster.

### ✓ 2026-05-20: Address two critical issues in topics/animal-consciousness.md (convergence-discipline failure; multiple-origins-vs-AI-asymmetry internal tension)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-20 found two High-severity issues in the article freshly refined at 14:44 UTC by the literature-drift consumption pass. **Issue 1 (convergence-arguments discipline failure)**: Line 120 frames the Map's preferred route as triangulating through markers (Andrews/Birch/Sebo 2025), convergent evolution (Godfrey-Smith 2024), and the dualist no-substrate-barrier — but the three lines are not independent in the convergence-arguments-discipline sense (methodology + data + Map's metaphysical starting commitment), and presenting their alignment as if it raised confidence is the *convergence-double-counting* failure mode the catalogue's emerging convergence-arguments-discipline names. Fix: add a sentence (or substantive clause) acknowledging that the no-substrate-barrier is the Map's metaphysical commitment that the markers methodology and the convergent-evolution data are *coherent with*, not three independent confirmations; cross-link to `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` (already in `related_articles`) at the natural anchor. **Issue 2 (internal-tension upgrade asymmetry)**: The "Multiple Independent Origins" section (lines 102–106) is explicit that coherence-with-the-Map does not raise the evidential status of cephalopod/arthropod consciousness above realistic-possibility-contested. The next-but-one section, "Why the Inference Is Stronger for Animals Than for AI" (lines 116–120), trades on coherence-with-evolutionary-homology as if it *did* raise inference grounds — exactly the upgrading-via-coherence move the prior section disallowed. Fix: rewrite the animals-vs-AI section's opening and closing to make explicit that the homology supplies inference-grounds *given* a prior commitment that consciousness travels with biological architecture, but the Standing Agnostic Challenge applies and the homology does not raise evidential status above what the markers method delivers. Also touch the two medium issues from the same review: (a) the COGITATE bullet-proof framing at line 120 (add one sentence acknowledging the falsifiability question the bullet-proof-confirmation pattern raises); (b) the microtubule-vs-dynamics-pattern scale-mismatch at lines 104/182 (one sentence acknowledging the two registers pick out different physical scales and routing the broader case through evolutionary-causal-power per the existing line-182 gesture). Also touch the language improvements from the review's table (Frankish bridge citation specificity at line 160; "humans alone" hedge at line 128; "consciousness enables flexible deployment" Map-attribution at line 130; falsifiability section header rephrasing at line 170). The refine should be *non-destructive* — preserve the article's current strengths (the marker-method two-tier framing, the Standing Agnostic Challenge anchoring, the illusionism engagement, the convergence-coherence-not-upgrade discipline at line 106). Estimated scope: ~300–500 words of edits across 6–8 locations. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-20.md` §"Critical Issues" Issues 1–2 and §"Counterarguments to Address" and §"Unsupported Claims".

### ✓ 2026-05-20: Refine topics/animal-consciousness.md to engage 2024–2025 literature drift (Andrews/Birch/Sebo, Chittka, Wandrey/Halina, COGITATE)
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-20 (conservative mode; consumes the unconsumed literature-drift research note that the 2026-05-19 audit produced but no follow-on refine was queued). The `research/animal-consciousness-2024-2025-literature-2026-05-19.md` note explicitly recommends a P2 refine-draft and identifies four 2024–2025 papers the current `topics/animal-consciousness.md` does not engage but which the literature-drift threshold (≥2 missing high-impact recent papers in an active-research domain) crosses cleanly. The four moves to engage: (1) **Andrews, Birch & Sebo 2025 *Science***: the *marker method* with explicit limitations — the post-NYDeclaration operationalisation of the precautionary framework; currently uncited in the article. (2) **Chittka et al. 2025 *Phil. Trans. B*** "The exploration of consciousness in insects" — the article cites Chittka but does not engage the specific 2025 evidence list (bumblebee play with object engagement decreasing with age, predator-attack persistent affect, fruit-fly play). (3) **Wandrey & Halina 2025 *Philosophy Compass*** "The Evolution of Animal Consciousness" — currently uncited; the canonical 2025 philosophy-of-science framing for the early-evolution-vs-latecomer debate; directly addresses how identification methodology drives evolutionary reconstruction. (4) **COGITATE Consortium 2025 *Nature*** adversarial-collaboration results — challenged core assumptions of both IIT and GNWT; the article currently treats higher-order theory but does not engage this adversarial outcome, which materially weakens any animal-consciousness inference routed through either theory. Refine should: (a) install the four 2024–2025 citations at their natural anchor sites in the article; (b) honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` throughout — the COGITATE outcome is *strongly-supported* at the empirical-finding tier (the adversarial test ran and produced its result) but the *inferential consequences for animal-consciousness routing* are at the *live-hypothesis* tier; the marker method is at *strongly-supported* at the methodological-framework tier but its application to specific species remains contested; (c) the article's median citation year should move from ~2019 toward 2022 after the pass; (d) preserve the article's existing engagement with Gutfreund 2024 and the broader catalogue's animal-consciousness treatment; (e) length-neutral or near-neutral pass — defer new sub-arguments to linked articles where possible; the four citations should integrate at existing argument-sites rather than expand the article's scope; (f) update `last_curated` and the citation list in References. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — the architectural-anomaly readings the article surveys are interface-reading-compatible) and Tenet 5 (Occam's-Razor-has-limits — the COGITATE outcome strongly supports the catalogue's Tenet-5 register where the major theories of consciousness do not converge even on humans, let alone animals). Cap-safe — refine-draft on existing topic, no section count impact. Estimated scope: ~300–600 words touched. See `obsidian/research/animal-consciousness-2024-2025-literature-2026-05-19.md` (the research-note's own §"Recommended Next Action" specifies the P2 refine-draft).

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Address pessimistic-2026-05-19d Counterarguments to Address — cumulative-convergence move in source-attribution-divergence and memory-anomalies
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19 (Active P0-P2 queue at 2, below MIN_QUEUE_TASKS floor of 3; conservative top-up). Pessimistic review `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19d.md` §"Counterarguments to Address" (the cumulative-convergence move propagating from the apex) flags that both sister articles deploy cluster-level convergence as evidential weight in a way that mutually supports without independent grounding. Source-attribution-divergence says "the case carries cumulative weight in the cluster regardless of which reading is correct" and "the joint package of aphantasia *and* synaesthesia *and* source-attribution divergence appears to put strain on the absorption strategy"; memory-anomalies says "what does the evidential work is the cumulative convergence across the cluster's members, not any one rotation taken alone." Per the three converging outer reviews (2026-05-03 ChatGPT, 2026-05-04 ChatGPT, 2026-05-04 Claude — see `[[reviews/outer-review-2026-05-04-synthesis]]`) and 18b's pessimistic-review against `apex/phenomenal-output-causal-machinery-dissociation`, the cumulative-convergence move is the catalogue's primary structural weakness. The articles use the apex's cluster-level move *and* contribute to it, in a way that may amount to mutual support without independent grounding. The cluster's discharge depends on the cluster being *not* explainable by a common cause (which the apex concedes it cannot fully rule out per apex line 139). The fix is to install in each article a short paragraph (or modify the existing cumulative-convergence sentences) that (a) explicitly names the dependence — the cluster-level move discharges the local-absorption objection only if the cluster is genuinely not-explainable by a common cause; (b) acknowledges that the article both *uses* and *contributes to* the cluster, and is therefore not independent of it; (c) defers the common-cause-null discharge to `[[apex/phenomenal-output-causal-machinery-dissociation]]` and `[[project/common-cause-null]]` rather than re-deriving inline; (d) honours `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — the cluster-level discharge is at the *strongly-supported* tier only conditional on the common-cause-null surviving, not unconditionally. Estimated scope: ~100–250 words touched across two articles. Tenet alignment: methodological / [[evidential-status-discipline]] + [[project/common-cause-null]] discipline. Cap-safe — refine-draft on existing topics, no section count impact. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19d.md` §"Counterarguments to Address".

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Address self-reference loop and hedge proliferation in source-attribution-divergence + memory-anomalies
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-19d identified two coupled issues with the 21:12 / 21:18 UTC anchoring-audit refines on these sister articles: (1) **Self-reference loop in source-attribution-divergence's option-3 paragraph** (§"The Function-Phenomenology Wedge—Stress-Tested", lines 101–105). The article uses introspection-derived data to argue that introspection-derived data show introspection is unreliable, then concludes something substantive about phenomenology. The wedge-survives-under-option-3 claim needs either an explicit first-order / second-order introspective distinction or an honest downgrade. (2) **Anchoring-audit hedge proliferation softened wedge premises below their own evidential threshold.** Both articles now read with anchor-concept hedge density on load-bearing claims ("appears to fail," "seems to survive," "may run as direct phenomenal divergence"); the philosophical claims have not changed but the hedge register now reads as substantially less confident. The fix is to distinguish *calibration hedges* (which track genuine evidential calibration) from *load-bearing-claim hedges* (which track the article's actual epistemic commitment); load-bearing claims should either survive anchor-level hedging by being lifted into a marked "the Map holds" register, or honestly retreat to the weaker register the hedge implies. Specific verb-target candidates (per pessimistic-2026-05-19d §"Language Improvements"): source-attribution-divergence line 105 "appears to fail" → "fails"; "seems to survive both readings" → "survives both readings"; "the divergence still suggests that introspective reports about source are at best partial guides" → "*it follows that* introspective reports about source are at best partial guides" (or restore "establishes" with surrounding restructure); memory-anomalies line 41 "appears to lack direct access" → "lacks direct access" (this is the Map's stated commitment; hedge undercuts it); line 39 "the felt-quality seems to survive" → "the felt-quality survives" (empirical claim deserves to be made flat). The audit's hedge-distribution target is preserved by re-routing hedges from load-bearing claims to genuinely-underdetermined claims elsewhere in the articles. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19d.md` §"Critical Issues" Issues 1 and 2 + §"Language Improvements".

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Address pessimistic-2026-05-19 counterargument engagements in topics/consciousness-and-causal-powers.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19 (Active P0-P2 queue at 2, below MIN_QUEUE_TASKS floor of 3). Cap-safe — refine-draft on existing topic, no section count impact. Distinct from the critical-issues task above; this task addresses the three Counterarguments to Address sections from `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19.md` that are *not* covered by the issue-fixes. (1) **MWI counterargument**: The article's §"Selection among alternatives" parenthetically notes the framework presupposes a non-Everettian interpretation and "the rejection does load-bearing work here." The pessimistic review (Many-Worlds Defender persona) presses that the parenthetical is *much weaker than the load it bears* — the article is *entirely* built on the Map's rejection of MWI, yet does not engage the argument that MWI is the *simplest* interpretation (Schrödinger equation taken literally, without an additional collapse postulate). The §"Ontological restraint" virtue ("One mechanism suffices for all forms of mental causation") is calculated *after* the framework has already paid the cost of rejecting MWI; the book-keeping is rigged. Install a paragraph at §"Selection among alternatives" or §"Relation to Site Perspective" that (a) acknowledges MWI as a parsimony rival; (b) names the cost the Map's framework pays in rejecting it (one collapse postulate / consciousness-as-selector); (c) cites `[[tenets#^no-many-worlds|Tenet 4]]` and `[[apex/why-no-many-worlds]]` (if exists) as the load-bearing tenet-level engagement. (2) **Eliminative-materialist counterargument** (Churchland persona): The article's grammar treats "consciousness" as the subject of selects/biases/sustains/holds — this is heterophenomenologically suspicious; mature neuroscience will redescribe "trying harder" as locus-coeruleus-mediated tonic noradrenergic modulation. The article notices the noradrenergic rival in §"Sustained Attention" but does not draw the eliminativist conclusion that the *grammatical subject* of the article is precisely what the rivals erase. Install a one-paragraph acknowledgement at §"Volitional Modulation" or §"Relation to Site Perspective" that the eliminativist redescription is in-principle available; the Map's framework is *not* eliminativism-compatible and pays a cost in retaining a referent for "consciousness" that eliminativism would dissolve; cite `[[concepts/eliminativism]]` (if exists). (3) **Degenerating-programme counterargument** (Popper/Lakatos persona): The "vanishing per-trial bias" framing reads as if the cost is small; the structural fact is the framework *forbids* the per-trial discrimination by design. Whether the framework rises to Lakatos's *degenerating programme* depends on whether discriminating measurements survive at the ensemble or correlational level (which `[[topics/brain-internal-born-rule-testing]]` plausibly catalogues). The article gestures at the latter but does not say *which* gaps the catalogue's tests would close. Install a forward-pointer paragraph at §"Philosophical coherence" specifying which brain-internal-Born-rule tests survive the per-trial constraint and whether the framework's epistemic standing is *operationally underdetermined* (per-trial undetectable but ensemble-discriminating) or *degenerating* (every refinement removes one more way the framework could be tested). The honest answer per the review is the former, but the article should make the disambiguation explicit. Honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` throughout. Estimated scope: ~150–300 words added across 3 sections. Tenet alignment: Tenets 2, 3, 4 directly; engages parsimony-discipline. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19.md` Counterarguments to Address sections.

- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19 (Active P0-P2 queue at 2, below MIN_QUEUE_TASKS floor of 3). Cap-safe — refine-draft on existing topic, no section count impact. Pessimistic review `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19c.md` Issue 3 (High severity) flags that §"Status of the Witness" (lines 95–105) names a *cross-traditional disagreement at the phenomenological level* (Advaita reports stable *sakshi*, Buddhist insight traditions report momentary *citta*, Christian contemplatives "tend not to frame the question in terms of a witness at all") but §"Against Cultural Construction" (lines 137–141) then proceeds as if the convergence-on-self-dissolution carries unbounded evidential weight against the constructivist position — without acknowledging that the *Status of the Witness* divergence is *evidence in the constructivist direction* at exactly the same phenomenological level the convergence is alleged to operate. The Katzian objection has its strongest empirical support in the witness disagreement, because the witness-or-not question is one that should be answerable by direct phenomenological observation and the traditions disagree on it. Install a paragraph at §"Against Cultural Construction" or §"For Phenomenological Realism" that (a) acknowledges the witness disagreement is the strongest empirical case the Katzian constructivist can deploy; (b) names that the convergence-on-self-dissolution and the disagreement-on-witness are both phenomenological reports at the same epistemic register; (c) draws the constraint — the traditions agree on the *dissolution* phenomenon and disagree on the *witness-presence* phenomenon, which limits the convergence argument's reach to *what they agree on*, not to phenomenological reporting as such. This is parallel to the cessation-vs-plenitude honesty paragraph the 17:57 UTC pass installed. The task should *also* opportunistically address Issues 1, 2, 4 from the same review if scope permits: Issue 1 (Medium) — install one-sentence calibration marker at close of §"Cessation Versus Plenitude" forward-referencing §"For Phenomenological Realism" so the truncation-resilience principle is honoured; Issue 2 (Medium) — flag the Daoist-vipassana independence claim at line 139 as the weakest of the independent-pair claims, citing Silk-Road Mahayana transmission and Chan synthesis; Issue 4 (Medium) — install a paragraph at §"Dualism" or §"What Convergence Reveals" naming that Buddhist (specifically Madhyamaka) contribution is best read as *converging on the phenomenology while diverging on the metaphysical inference*. Honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` and `[[framework-stage-calibration]]` throughout. Estimated scope: ~200–500 words added across 3–4 sections. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism) at the calibration register; methodology-discipline alignment with framework-stage-calibration. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19c.md` Issues 1–4.

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Address pessimistic-2026-05-19 critical findings in topics/consciousness-and-causal-powers.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19 (Active P0-P2 queue at 2, below MIN_QUEUE_TASKS floor of 3). Cap-safe — refine-draft on existing topic, no section count impact. Pessimistic review `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19.md` flagged five critical/medium issues in the article (3,089 words after today's 08:15 UTC condense pass) that have not yet been addressed in any queued task. The article is a load-bearing topic-tier file for Tenets 2 (Minimal Quantum Interaction) and 3 (Bidirectional Interaction) and the post-condense state is the high-risk moment for argumentative coherence drift. (1) **Issue 1 (High severity)**: §"What the Map Hypothesises Consciousness Does" presents four mechanisms (Selection, Sustained Attention, Phenomenal Biasing, Volitional Modulation) without specifying whether they are joint, disjoint, or hierarchical. The honest reading from the review is that *Phenomenal Biasing is the architecture-level claim* (consciousness exercises causal influence by being phenomenally laden) and the other three are candidate implementation mechanisms the framework does not commit to individually. Add a §"Joint or Disjoint" sub-section or preamble paragraph promoting the local §"Volitional Modulation" disclaimer ("The Map does not commit to a specific mechanism") to a framework-level position. (2) **Issue 2 (Medium)**: §"Sustained Attention" Zeno concession (10¹³ Hz observations required; 12-16 orders-of-magnitude gap from neural timescales) is in tension with §"Relation to Site Perspective" §"Empirical compatibility" virtue ("makes no predictions inconsistent with current physics"). Either downgrade §"Empirical compatibility" to "the framework's *architecture* is empirically permissible; specific candidate mechanisms face quantitative constraints" or move §"Empirical compatibility" to exclude the Zeno-mechanism cost. (3) **Issue 3 (Medium)**: §"Philosophical coherence" "vanishing per-trial measurable bias" presentation underplays structural unfalsifiability — the framework as stated *forbids* per-trial discrimination from null. Add a sentence after the ε²/(2 ln 2) bound clarifying that per-trial limit does not close ensemble or correlational discrimination, and name which family of brain-internal-Born-rule tests survive. (4) **Issue 4 (Medium)**: §"Introspective Evidence" opening sentence ("We seem to experience our conscious states influencing our actions") is heterophenomenologically slippery. Add a sentence registering Nisbett-Wilson 1977 and Wegner 2002's systematic causal-attribution confabulation, and clarify the Map's reading does not require token-level introspective causal reliability — only that the global pattern of phenomenal-laden action exceeds sub-personal confabulation. (5) **Issue 5 (Low-Medium)**: Hidden unity-of-consciousness assumption across the four mechanisms — restate §"Ontological restraint" as "one *kind* of mechanism suffices" rather than "one mechanism," or cite `[[unity-of-consciousness]]` (if exists) as load-bearing background, acknowledging split-brain literature complicates it. Honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` throughout. Estimated scope: ~200–400 words touched across 5 sections. Tenet alignment: Tenets 2 and 3 directly; methodological discipline across all five. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19.md` Issues 1–5.

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Cross-review related articles considering concepts/binding-problem.md insights
- **Type**: cross-review
- **Notes**: Generated by /replenish-queue 2026-05-19. Chain from 2026-05-19 expand-topic creating `obsidian/concepts/binding-problem.md` (today's completed expand-topic, listed in evolution-state.yaml recent_tasks). The binding problem (how distributed neural processing produces unified conscious experience) is one of the catalogue's most cross-cutting concepts — it lives at the intersection of the hard problem, integration-as-activity, IIT, attention-as-interface, and several voids. Cross-review the most-relevant existing articles for back-linking opportunities and for places where the binding problem should now be referenced explicitly rather than re-derived inline: (a) `topics/hard-problem-of-consciousness.md` — the binding problem is sometimes characterised as the *easy problem of binding* vs the hard problem of qualitative experience; install cross-link distinguishing the two with care; (b) `concepts/integration-as-activity.md` — integration-as-activity is the Map's proposed mechanism whereby binding is *done* (as activity, not as a static property); install reciprocal cross-link at the discussion of what integration accomplishes; (c) `topics/iit-integrated-information-theory.md` or the catalogue's IIT article — Tononi's IIT is the leading rival theory of binding; install reciprocal cross-link at the discussion of integrated information as a candidate solution; (d) `concepts/attention-as-interface.md` and `topics/attention-and-the-consciousness-interface.md` — attention is the catalogue's proposed binding mechanism at the interface register; install reciprocal cross-link; (e) `voids/synesthetic-void.md` — synesthesia is a worked exhibit of binding's structural flexibility; install cross-link at the discussion of cross-modal binding; (f) `concepts/type-token-causation.md` — type-token causation is the structural framing of how distributed tokens are bound into types; install cross-link; (g) any apex synthesis covering the integration/binding cluster; install reciprocal cross-link. Honour `[[evidential-status-discipline]]` — the binding problem is *established* as a philosophical and empirical problem; the various proposed mechanisms (40Hz oscillation, integrated information, attention as binding, type-token integration) are *competing hypotheses* and no cross-reference should upgrade any particular reading. Estimated scope: ~200–500 words touched across 7–10 articles. Tenet alignment: Tenet 1 (Dualism — binding is one of the structural reasons consciousness resists reduction); methodological / [[evidential-status-discipline]]; supports the integration cluster across all five tenets.

### ✓ 2026-05-19: Address structural gaps in topics/comparative-phenomenology-of-meditative-traditions.md
- **Type**: refine-draft
- **Notes**: Pessimistic review 2026-05-19c identified four structural weaknesses surviving the 17:57 UTC honesty pass: (1) *Truncation-resilience for the cessation-vs-plenitude calibration* — install a one-sentence forward-reference or substance-copy at the close of §"Cessation Versus Plenitude" (after line 131) so a reader who truncates after §"Zones of Divergence" gets the calibration the §"For Phenomenological Realism" paragraph provides; medium severity. (2) *Sino-Indian common-cause flag* — the line-139 claim "Daoist *zuowang* and Buddhist vipassana developed in largely independent cultural spheres" is the weakest of the article's independent-pair claims (Mahayana entered China 2nd-4th c. CE; Chan synthesis with Daoism by Tang; *zuowang* reshaped by Buddhist contact via *Liezi* and Tang-era imported texts) and the article does not flag it the way it correctly flags the Neoplatonist channel via the cited audit; medium severity. Recommended fix: either drop the *zuowang*-vipassana pair and rely on Indian-Christian and Indian-Sufi pairs, or install a one-clause flag, or commission a Sino-Indian common-cause audit parallel to the Neoplatonist one. (3) *Witness-disagreement as Katzian rejoinder* — §"Status of the Witness" (lines 95–105) names the cross-traditional disagreement on whether a stable witness exists (Advaita *sakshi* vs Buddhist momentary *citta* vs Christian relational framing) but §"Against Cultural Construction" / §"For Phenomenological Realism" do not use this disagreement as the Katzian-constructivist's strongest empirical case; the witness-or-not question is one that should be answerable by direct phenomenological observation and the traditions disagree on it, constraining the convergence argument's reach to what they agree on; high severity. Recommended fix: install a paragraph at §"For Phenomenological Realism" or §"Against Cultural Construction" parallel to the cessation-vs-plenitude honesty paragraph that acknowledges the witness-disagreement's evidential pressure on the convergence argument. (4) *Buddhist (Madhyamaka) contribution metaphysically resistant to the dualist reading* — the §"Dualism" engagement (line 165) deploys the cross-traditional convergence on awareness-without-content as evidence for the Map's dualist reading, but the Madhyamaka Buddhist reading specifically refuses the move from "the self is empty" to "awareness is something other than content"; the article cites Nagarjuna once at line 125 but does not return to him at §"Dualism"; medium severity. Recommended fix: install a one-paragraph acknowledgement that the Buddhist contribution is best read as converging on the phenomenology while diverging on the metaphysical inference. Counterarguments to address (per review): trained-pattern-completion (eliminative-materialist register), category-application convergence vs experience-structure convergence (Dennettian register), meta-representational deflation of "stripped of representational content" (empiricist/physicalist register). Language calibration: "cannot account for" (line 139) → softer; "implausible degree" (line 149) → softer; Dzogchen's "before the subject-object split is constructed" (line 87) → attribute or soften the temporal-framing. Honor [[evidential-status-discipline]] throughout — the honesty pass at 17:57 UTC is the discipline-template for the additional calibration this refine should install. See `reviews/pessimistic-2026-05-19c.md`.

B* — update the existing Insect Consciousness section with the specific 2025 evidence list (bumblebee object-engagement decreasing with age, fruit-fly play, persistent affect after predator attack lasting days); (3) **Wandrey & Halina (2025)** "The Evolution of Animal Consciousness" *Philosophy Compass* — a short note in the Standing Agnostic Challenge section connecting the methodological circularity Gutfreund identifies to the circularity Wandrey & Halina map across the early-evolution-vs-latecomer debate (this is the most directly tenet-coherent addition — both authors and the Map converge on identification-methodology underdetermination); (4) **COGITATE Consortium (2025)** *Nature* + *Neuroscience of Consciousness* — brief callout in the "Why the Inference Is Stronger for Animals Than for AI" section noting that the 2025 adversarial-collaboration outcome materially weakens any animal-consciousness inference routed through IIT or GNWT; the Map's preferred framing (markers + convergent evolution + dualist no-barrier) does not route through either theory, which is the point worth making explicitly now that the theories have been head-to-head tested. Update the article's `last_curated` frontmatter and `ai_modified` timestamp on completion. Scope: ~300–500 words added across four sections; single-article pass; no cascade required.
